Object 42.title: Object 42
Object 42.description:
Spice of Life

Description

Spice of Life is an anomalous substance that resembles fine green glitter. It is most commonly found stored in small cylindrical containers, each holding approximately 50 grams of material. The majority of recovered containers lack printed labels with labeled examples reading:

Spice of Life
A Tezo’s Art Supplies product

Alongside a flaky form, a glitter-infused liquid comparable in texture to decorative glue exists. This form is stored in squeeze tubes with a single nozzle and functions similarly to common paint or adhesive products. All unused containers of Spice of Life consistently appear in different levels. Environmental hazards such as moisture, heat, or corrosion do not appear to affect the substance.

Spice of Life most commonly appear in levels that display child-like activities or artistic vision. However not exclusive to these areas, Spice of Life has a much higher frequency of appearance in those levels. Additionally, Object 42 will often appear in groups of 3 or more. While a maximum amount is not known, the current highest cluster of Spice of Life was 10 of them, 6 normal bottles, and 4 tubes. The reason for it is unknown.

Current studies show that bottled versions of Object 42 have a 1.4x greater chance to appear than the tube counterpart, despite them sharing very few differences. All bottles and tubes of Spice of Life, unless previously used, appear in mint condition, and can even be unaffected by environmental dangers until interacted with.

Properties
The main use of Spice of Life is a healing agent. When Object 42 interacts with wounds or blood, it's glitter-like compound dissolves into the flesh and stimulate grown in that specific region until the wound is completely sealed off. These healed areas will maintain a greenish sparkle to the area, lasting roughly 30 days until fading away. During this period of time, wanderers have stated how these sections feel much stronger and much more able in comparison to how it was previously, which has lead Spice of Life to be considered for other injuries, though testing on these have been canceled.

An overuse of Spice of Life in a region can develop cysts within the area, many have a bright green hue to them. These cysts take significantly longer to fade away than its normal life span, and will amass in count, size, and healing time as more is applied. Attempting to damage or pop these malformations only causes further damage to the flesh. Doing this can cause Spice of Life to fail working, instead getting eaten up by the skin and the wound reappearing. As mentioned previously, larger cysts will lead to more damage, with additional (and older) wounds also having the possibility to be reopened if they are destroyed.

Outside of direct appliance on damaged flesh, its use is highly dangerous to the body. No matter where it lands, Spice of Life will produce skin. This means actions such as digesting it, and placing it in ones eyes will cause permanent damage to the area, often having the area covered up by flesh. Deaths as a result from this are few, but have occurred. The medical operations required to remove such skin growths often are logistically impossible, leaving many with damage to their body that will never recover.

History

Spice of Life was in-development from the mid-1600s. At this period, Tezo's Art Supplies was trying to study figamorphism and understand how Lycheens used it to create various resources. Ethel Tezo and the first team of art researchers made their first prototype which was named “Spice of Laugh”.

From 1950, after the group modernized, Tezo's Art Supplies brought back the development of the renamed Spice of Life. The research team of Tezo contacted F.L.O.R.A. that provided carefully nurtured plant samples, mineral-rich soil samples, and data related to parameters that were stable in agriculture. A consistent version of Spice of Life was reached by 1986. The compound when used on open wounds was known to seal the wounds by growing layers of skin and healed them off fast. Though not planned in the beginning the glitter-like appearance proved to have contributory role in activation and consistency. This contribution was done by a leading artist at the time and presently one of the Onsite Expert Comittee, Todd Rumernick.

Spice of Laugh

Although Spice of Laugh began as an early prototype, it was not discarded after the development of Spice of Life. Later tests showed that the original compound gave a cool sensation and made patients feel happier when used on injuries. While these effects were considered secondary at the time, they were consistent enough to warrant further study.

By the early 1990s, Spice of Laugh was reintroduced as a separate, secondary product. Unlike Spice of Life, it does not induce regenerative growth. It reduced stress, elevated mood, and comfortable than applying Spice of Life on the injury.

Spice of Laugh resembles a bright blue, glitter-glue. It is stored in squeeze bottles. Unlike Spice of Life, it appears alone. In documented rare clusters containing ten containers of Spice of Life, two additional containers of Spice of Laugh are often found alongside them.

═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: Helena Kiena, current savant of Tezo’s Art Supplies

═ᗌ DamnSorcerer_0: They told me it isn’t science. Are they bullshitting?
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: Sort of.
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: We know that, that in baseline reality, there exists rules that cannot be broken. An apple is an apple, no matter what name you give it.
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: Our home, doesn’t follow that rule, however. And it’s not through formulas or fancy tech.
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: It’s rules. Life doesn’t follow the rules. It bends and contorts and makes itself anew.
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: If you want to become an artist, you have to learn to bend those rules.
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: If you give it the right suggestions. Help it connect the dots, suddenly the apple is an orange.
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: And once you know how to do it, suddenly reality becomes a lot less incomprehensible.
═ᗌ 🖌SpicerLifer: Incredible
═ᗌ ★xX_Gaymer_Xx: Absolutely showstopping
═ᗌ DamnSorcerer_0: Yea I’m not reading all of that.
═ᗌ ✎JeffTheJiller: Your loss.

Object 40.title: Object 40
Object 40.description:
Corrosive Bubbles are groups of reflective monochromatic bubbles inhabiting dangerous and sometimes natural aquatic levels, specifically small isolated bodies of water of any other liquids. Corrosive Bubbles are notable for their dangerous properties, causing inflammation, a stinging pain, or tissue death upon touch; organ death if consumed or inserted into one's blood, and various amounts of auditory hallucinations or effects it can cause when it is in proximity to a wanderer.

Corrosive Bubbles form naturally, commonly in moderately bacteria-affected water, water or Almond Water that has been still for a prolonged amount of time, briefly in [ DATA LOST ].  But in common cases, it can spontaneously start forming quickly into big groups of Corrosive Bubbles. These huge groups have shown a low level of predatory intelligence and stealth, able to identify the closest wanderer within a 100-meter radius and to in some cases, stop making their natural noises to attack a wanderer silently.

Attempts to destroy Corrosive Bubbles via forced means have not been feasible; observations have rather shown that the object can only be destroyed through natural means, such as exceeding its lifespan , rare cases of spontaneous disappearances, extreme growth of microorganisms, constant movement or somehow forced into its body of water.

As mentioned initially, Corrosive Bubbles has been shown to cause auditory hallucinations/effects in some close encounters. While most of the reports are inconsistent with what auditory hallucinations it causes; it can range from a single note echoing for ~5 seconds, water trickles from Level 37, quiet explosions, short bursts of low pitches playing, sudden heavy reverberation or echoing, clarity shifts, transitionary pitch shifts, and in unconfirmed cases, harmonic changes, timbre shifts, or all of the mentioned auditory effects briefly occurring.

The object has a numerous amount of minor properties that are listed rather then documented in one paragraph.
*In huge quantities, Corrosive Bubbles can drift slowly at 30 meters per hour.
*Slows down microorganism growth.
*Can harm entities, generally causing lacerations, burns, and wounds.
*Can dilute vital liquids and one will not feel the effects of the Corrosive Bubbles.
*Has been observed to consume their dead victims in some cases.
*Has been observed to change its properties ever since it was discovered.
*Cannot merge with other instances.

Object 38.title: Object 38
Object 38.description:
Object 38

"DOOM LOCKS"

Object 38

"DOOM LOCKS"

Description
Object 38, known as Doom Locks, are electronic keypad-based door locks found in slightly treacherous hallway-like levels of the Backrooms, including, but not limited to, Level 21, Level 26, and Level 42. The lock's keypad is at the top of the device, with the doorknob in the middle, and a hidden screen is at the bottom. Once a wanderer interacts with the keypad or doorknob, the lock's bottom screen displays the 1993 game Doom, with a small speaker for playing the game's audio. Once the wanderer finishes the game, the door unlocks. The door then leads wanderers to random levels, usually safe havens such as Level 330, Level 399, Level 98, and many others.

Finding Doom Locks is notably challenging, even in levels where it manifests. Assuming wanderers are in the desired level and travel nonstop while never reentering previously visited areas, wanderers would take approximately three to seven days to find a Doom Lock. In levels unaffected by Phenomenon 15, instances of Doom Locks are visible to only one wanderer unless interacted with. Once the lock unlocks and the wanderer enters through the door, it will manifest for another wanderer, and the lock will not be visible or usable to the previous individual who used it.

In the Frontrooms, it would take an average person 5 hours to beat Classic Doom. However, four leading factors increase the average time needed to finish the version in Doom Locks. Firstly, the wanderer has to crouch slightly to see the bottom screen, increasing discomfort and possibly causing spinal issues. Secondly, one must use the keypad to control the player character in the game, which would take ample time to adapt to, especially in the position mentioned earlier. Thirdly, the screen is too diminutive for a wanderer to process thoroughly, which causes many failures in runs, further driving up playtime. Lastly, most wanderers are unfamiliar with playing first-person shooters, notably Classic Doom. These factors drive up the time to unlock a Doom Lock to an average of 9 hours.

In addition, the audio emanating from a Doom Lock and the wanderer standing still defenseless increases their susceptibility to hostile entities. Standing still for nine hours depletes a wanderer's resources, mainly sustenance. If the Doom Lock's keypad remains untouched for five minutes, it will reset to the beginning, giving wanderers a small window to perform other actions. For every reset, the game's difficulty, which starts at "Hey, not too rough," increases, and the subsequent window's time is reduced by 30 seconds. The lock has a maximum of three resets, and the Doom Lock bricks when reaching the 4th reset.

Doom Locks are indestructible, and so are their respective doors. In addition, attempting to interfere with the lock's software through any means results in the bricking of any involved devices, including the lock itself. Attempting to toy with a lock while another wanderer is using it will also result in its bricking. Bricked Doom Locks vanish when the wanderer looks away for five seconds. Opening a door that had a bricked lock sends one to The Void, or in semi-rare instances, The Broken.

The discovery of Doom Locks dates back to July 5, 2020, when some wanderers in Level 42 reported hearing faint noises of the song At Doom's Gate, the most popular OST for the game Doom. A few days later, wanderers found the first lock in the level. It was not until July 31, 2020, when the first Doom Lock was unlocked, which led the individual to Level 399. Rumors about the object leading wanderers to safe havens spread rapidly, and many wanderers wanted to attempt unlocking it, with many ending in failure. Today, Doom Locks are an infamous method to potentially access safe havens, although not recommended by masses due to the many dangers.

Once the wanderer finishes the game, the door unlocks. The door then leads wanderers to random levels, usually safe havens such as Level 330, Level 399, Level 98, and many others.

Finding Doom Locks is notably challenging, even in levels where it manifests. Assuming wanderers are in the desired level and travel nonstop while never reentering previously visited areas, wanderers would take approximately three to seven days to find a Doom Lock. In levels unaffected by Phenomenon 15, instances of Doom Locks are visible to only one wanderer unless interacted with. Once the lock unlocks and the wanderer enters through the door, it will manifest for another wanderer, and the lock will not be visible or usable to the previous individual who used it.

In the Frontrooms, it would take an average person 5 hours to beat Classic Doom. However, four leading factors increase the average time needed to finish the version in Doom Locks. Firstly, the wanderer has to crouch slightly to see the bottom screen, increasing discomfort and possibly causing spinal issues. Secondly, one must use the keypad to control the player character in the game, which would take ample time to adapt to, especially in the position mentioned earlier. Thirdly, the screen is too diminutive for a wanderer to process thoroughly, which causes many failures in runs, further driving up playtime. Lastly, most wanderers are unfamiliar with playing first-person shooters, notably Classic Doom. These factors drive up the time to unlock a Doom Lock to an average of 9 hours.

In addition, the audio emanating from a Doom Lock and the wanderer standing still defenseless increases their susceptibility to hostile entities. Standing still for nine hours depletes a wanderer's resources, mainly sustenance. If the Doom Lock's keypad remains untouched for five minutes, it will reset to the beginning, giving wanderers a small window to perform other actions. For every reset, the game's difficulty, which starts at "Hey, not too rough," increases, and the subsequent window's time is reduced by 30 seconds. The lock has a maximum of three resets, and the Doom Lock bricks when reaching the 4th reset.

Doom Locks are indestructible, and so are their respective doors. In addition, attempting to interfere with the lock's software through any means results in the bricking of any involved devices, including the lock itself. Attempting to toy with a lock while another wanderer is using it will also result in its bricking. Bricked Doom Locks vanish when the wanderer looks away for five seconds. Opening a door that had a bricked lock sends one to The Void, or in semi-rare instances, The Broken.

The discovery of Doom Locks dates back to July 5, 2020, when some wanderers in Level 42 reported hearing faint noises of the song At Doom's Gate, the most popular OST for the game Doom. A few days later, wanderers found the first lock in the level. It was not until July 31, 2020, when the first Doom Lock was unlocked, which led the individual to Level 399. Rumors about the object leading wanderers to safe havens spread rapidly, and many wanderers wanted to attempt unlocking it, with many ending in failure. Today, Doom Locks are an infamous method to potentially access safe havens, although not recommended by masses due to the many dangers.

Shiny Nickles (Joke).title: Shiny Nickles (Joke)
Shiny Nickles (Joke).description:
Shiny Nickles look like nickels found within the Frontrooms. But they aren't. Shiny Nickles are far shinier and worth more than their American counterpart. While American nickels are worth 5 cents, Shiny Nickles are worth 7 cents. This major difference would disrupt the American economy if they were ever introduced, so they were dumped into the backrooms on July 4th, 1976 in the "Great Nickle Transit".

After all the Shiny Nickles went into the Backrooms, they became a form of currency. Because of the finite number of Shiny Nickles in the Backrooms, pricing was unruly and difficult to pin down, and those who found the piles of Shiny Nickles earlier tended to hoard that wealth to the point of murder. Regardless, it was clear that Shiny Nickles would cause a collapse in the backrooms society.

We live in a society where profit is religion. To attack it is to be sinful. We love money. And we love using money, to make more money. Money is the vehicle of every society, so making more money is the goal of the common man. But man is also lazy, so they want money fast and with minimal effort. And do you know what is fast and ends quickly? me

However. God does not like you, so you loose all your Shiny Nickles to the casino. This happened to basically everyone teehee, no one could use their Shiny Nickles anymore. At first, most people were sad. Money no longer existed, so the world had to rely on a bartering economy again. No more were the days of fixed prices and clear value. They were brought back down to the level of cavemen. Just unga bunga until the day they died, likely from a Smiler.

But out of nowhere, everything would change. On youtube, March 1st, 1984, a video would come out: "I worked for Mr Moolah, he's a fraud"

This 30 hour expose of the cheating of every single slot machine, roulette wheel, and other gambling devices brought fury to the righteous addicts. They thought their money was lost fair and square, but no! It was whisked away under their large noses, now owned by the man with the greatest nose, Mr Cash £ Moolah (Yes "£" is his middle name). Infuriated men and women across the Backroom revolted, and mass movement through levels ended up cluttering the bounds of reality as everyone wanted to find that swine. And when they did, the wanderers tore him to pieces. Making sure none of his gaudy clothing remained.

Yet they made on final and foolish mistake. Breaking Mr Moolah's remote. This was the only way to get into the impenetrable vault deep within his casino empire. They were consumed by greed so great, they shot themselves in the foot. We live in a society where our rage dictates our behavior, not any sort of reason or goodness. And where did it leave them? With nothing! While many have tried to make new currencies to replace their Shiny Nickles (such as Shiny Nickle Crypto, Shiny Penies, and Dirty Nickles), nothing worked like the Nickles did.

For now, the vault remains as a sad tale of greed and the foolishness of man to see themselves as better and smarter than the rest. A testament to the failures of mob mentality and how easy it is to be convinced by others. The casino is still used, just now they use almond water to pour small bits into the slots and roulette to bet. Don't ask how it works, they tend to get violent over it.

There is no lesson here. We just live in a society.

By Mr Moolah's stupid son, and "greenlit" by Mr Moolah's wife.

BASILISK.title: BASILISK
BASILISK.displayTitle: 𝙱 𝙰 𝚂 𝙸 𝙻 𝙸 𝚂 𝙺
BASILISK.description:
BASILISK is a sophisticated piece of fileless 'Swiss Army Knife' malware developed by  ████ ███ during our 2019 Hackathon, though it later saw use during the Cascadan Conflict as per a commission from A-2 for large-scale cyberattacks on the Cascadan government. The virus is by far our most prideful creation, with it encompassing a wide range of malware types such as worms, ransomware, botnets, spyware, scareware, rootkits, and keyloggers within its various payloads. It also utilizes psychology by subverting various facets of traditional malware to create a false sense of security within the infected indivdual, essentially making BASILISK explicitly hide itself in plain sight and toy with the terminal it infects as its method of secrecy. I know that's a bit excessive for a virus deployed with the express purpose of government infiltration, but we already had it, and we didn't really feel like making something new, so we just rolled with it and got lazy. Don't tell the Anti Entity Group that we did that, though—they still think we made BASILISK for them.

While incredibly impressive on a functional level, on a technical level, BASILISK is pretty scuffed; it was written incredibly fast for the hackathon. That was a fun month devoid of sleep, and because of this, the virus only works on BackOS or BackOS-adjacent/BackOS-based systems, and the code can be best described as the greatest piece of Italian spaghetti known to man, so if you're looking forward to dousing your eyes in Liquid Pain this morning (The Supplier has a bunch of it, so you can just get some; don't ask), be my guest and look at the source code on our repo. Also, stop making BASILISK forks and then not adding anything; you're driving [planning gal] EntropycaCardine insane.
BASILISK.lead:
I.R.I.S. | Infiltrate. Manipulate. Sabotage.

Last edited August 17th, 2022 by [data mannie] crestfallen_

Shiny Dollers (Joke).title: Shiny Dollers (Joke)
Shiny Dollers (Joke).description:
At first glance, Shiny Dollers appear to be a laminated $1 dollar bill. Yet their looks deceive you. Shiny Dollers have been polished to a bright shine, making them worth $1.50 instead of the traditional $1 dollar bill. When they were first minted in 1975, the president declared them to be destroyed, as if they were put into circulation, it would cause extreme inflation. Following this, on July 4th, 1976, the Shiny Dollers were dumped into the backrooms in the “Great Doller Transit”

When the Shiny Dollers were first discovered in the backrooms, they were immediately adopted as currency. Like the previous Shiny Nickles, which had a finite amount, only 2,587 Shiny Dollers were discovered. Even when the backrooms was caught up in the Shiny Nickle craze, they still found a way to drag the Shiny Dollers into it, and entire wars were fought over two or three Shiny Dollers.

Casinos at the time didn’t want to accept such a currency, as it would attract unwanted attention from street gangs and criminals looking to steal the Shiny Dollers. The masses pushed and pushed and pushed to have Shiny Dollers accepted at casinos and even advocated for a Shiny Doller area, specifically dedicated to having machines and tables where only Shiny Dollers were accepted. Eventually, the people of the backrooms threatened to stop going to the casinos altogether, and then the casinos cracked under pressure.

You and others flooded into the casino on opening day, with your life savings of two Shiny Dollers. But, Tyche did not smile upon you that fateful day. You gambled all your Shiny Dollers away on a Blackjack hand. You stayed with 14. The next card was a 7. Eventually by the end of the day, everyone had lost all their Shiny Dollers to the casino. The backrooms turned back to Shiny Nickles, but they had fallen that day as well. The backrooms had to devolve back to a simple bartering system, just like the day of its inception.

But out of nowhere, everything changed in a backrooms-wide upset. On March 1st, 1984, a former employee of the casino came forward in a video posted on YouTube.

The video was 30 hours long, carefully going through all the unfair tricks the casino implemented in all the slot machines, roulette wheels, and even decks of cards. When the gamblers who had lost their dollers and nickles that day discovered the video, they were instantly overcome with rage. They all had thought their nickles and dollers were lost fairly that day. Avarice blinded them, they assumed. But no. Their money was whisked away by Mr. Cash ₤ Moolah (Yes, ₤ is his middle name) All the gamblers, enraged by this diabolical act of pure greed, scoured the backrooms for any sign of him, cluttering up the bounds of reality. And when they did, they showed no mercy in destroying him, and even his huge mansion, making sure not even any rubble or shreds of his exorbitantly priced clothes remained.

But avarice had blinded them yet again. In the frenzy of death and destruction, they had failed to notice one thing. They had broken Mr. Moolah’s remote. It was the only thing that could open up the virtually impenetrable vault where all their Nickles and Dollers now permanently resided. The gamblers flew into another fit of rage, but soon realized it was meaningless. Many of the enraged gamblers attempted to develop other currencies to replace their dollers and nickles, such as Shiny Nickle Crypto, Shiny Penies, Dirty Nickles, and even Creased Dollers, nothing ever managed to take the place of the nickles and dollers.

Now, the vault forever stands as a reminder of the greed humans exhibit when they find something their mind makes them feel an everlasting sense of want for. The gamblers neglected to destroy the casino. Instead, Mr. Moolah’s grandson took control and modified all the slot machines and even the tables to accept Almond Water as a new form of currency.

Yet again, there’s no lesson here. We just live in a society.

Object 49.title: Object 49
Object 49.description:
DESCRIPTION

Object 49, hereby referred to as Wallpaper Marshmallows, are foul-tasting sweets that take the appearance of an unassuming marshmallow and have a very distinct vanilla scent. Wallpaper Marshmallows have been given their name due to them tasting similar to their namesake and the fact that instances of Wallpaper Marshmallows are often found within metallic bowls behind a level's wallpaper. They are often a source of last-minute sustenance for lost wanderers deprived of food.

Despite Wallpaper Marshmallows often being found within the deteriorating walls of most indoor levels, they are always pristine and lack any remnants from the wall or its insulation, regardless of whether or not debris from the damaged wall should land on the bowl or otherwise. This phenomenon is paired with the anomaly of bowls of Wallpaper Marshmallows only manifesting as soon as there is damage within a wall that would lead to the bowl being visible.

Contrary to their foul taste, Wallpaper Marshmallows are perfectly fine for human consumption in small amounts. Excessive consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows can potentially cause damage to one's stomach. Furthermore, unnecessary consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows should be avoided unless it is simply used as an ingredient in a larger meal.

Wallpaper Marshmallows have an extremely filling effect regardless of whether or not one's stomach is actually full to the brim. This effect has led to Wallpaper Marshmallows being used as an additive within dying settlements to keep their members happy. Unrestrained consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows can additionally lead to starvation, as one believes they are satiated and do not need to eat.

Wallpaper Marshmallows found outside of a wall or large settlements should be avoided due to the possibility of them being laced with dangerous substances such as Liquid Pain or Cashew Water, this also applies to collections of Wallpaper Marshmallows outside of the usual metallic bowls they are normally found in. Additionally, one should avoid consumption of Wallpaper Marshmallows with a more pungent smell opposed to the usual sweet scent.

Attempting to melt Wallpaper Marshmallows or providing unnecessary heat will lead to a chemical reaction within the marshmallow making consumption unsafe. This chemical reaction is irreversible, and affected Wallpaper Marshmallows can be identified with their discoloration into a yellow hue. While the change is irreversible, its effects can be negated. Gradually supplying melted Wallpaper Marshmallows with drops of Almond Water can make affected instances relatively safe to consume. This technique is often used in settlements producing products using Wallpaper Marshmallows, though can be used if one accidentally applied heat to a bowl of marshmallows.

Sweetened Wallpaper Marshmallows have been used in a plethora of recipes within the bases of M.E.G. and several other groups to keep morale high during problematic times. When low on supplies, several groups have been known to unnecessarily add Wallpaper Marshmallows to keep their members feeling full in order to avoid complaints and strain on the food supply.

Wallpaper Marshmallows are occasionally found within the flooring of certain levels, usually drenched in [ DATA LOST ] or other substances that would make the affected instances unsafe for consumption. The appearance of Wallpaper Marshmallows within flooring is believed to be completely natural, as—like regular instances—there are no signs of damage besides the contamination from [ DATA LOST ].

APPLICATIONS

Wallpaper Marshmallows are usually reserved to being a quick yet disgusting snack whilst exploring or as an ingredient in a variety of recipes. As mentioned previously, Wallpaper Marshmallows are occasionally used as an additive by dying settlements attempting to keep their members full. Many groups utilize Wallpaper Marshmallows in M.R.E.'s during times of war, the filling effect of the marshmallows preventing soldiers from not preforming as well due to hunger pains.

FOOTNOTES

----

Object 11.title: Object 11
Object 11.description:
Wentworth Gas is a mystical and dangerous chemical found in air that is known to impair a wanderer's health. It looks foggier than regular water vapour, but is completely odourless. It is believed to be highly magnetic and usually found in areas with strong electromagnetic fields, such as powered electric engines. Its L.D.50 was found to be 560 mg/kg, slightly more dangerous than methamphetamine from the Frontrooms.

The condensation point for Wentworth Gas is 253 K (-20 °C). This implies that in cold-temperature levels, Wentworth Gas could naturally be found as in liquid state. Understandably, none has been found yet, mainly because of the difficulties in studying such cold levels. Its freezing point is 173 K (-100 °C). None has been found, either.

ETD-8 AVMH.title: ETD-8 AVMH
ETD-8 AVMH.description:
ETD-8 Asquistea Verus di Mediu Hostilies (AVMH)

Asquistea Verus di Mediu Hostilies (translated from Aurielleno: Hostile Environment Combat Uniform) (HECU, AVMH) is protective gear used by primarily Auriellen military personnel in hazardous and toxic environments, such as during chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear (CBRN) strikes, and levels classified as Class 4 and above. It is used by the Auriellen Armed Forces, Cascadan Army, and Rama Saom's Security Department Rapid Response Unit, Strategic Deployment Force, and Special Tasks Squadron.

The AVMH encompasses four parts: the Costem Liero Protela Integratem per Medius Pericluvos (CLPIMP), gas mask, chemical agent detector paper, gloves, and overboots. Additionally, various items, mainly protective vests such as the AGF's IOTV, Cascadan Army's LBE Vest, and others, are doctrinally worn over the CLIMP
ETD-8 AVMH.lead:

Object 60.title: Object 60
Object 60.description:
Corrosion Repellent is a highly elusive and valuable industrial substance due to its rarity to obtain, and if it is easy to obtain, the environment in which it is found in is often very dangerous. It is typically stored in industrial-grade spray cans that are completely blank, lacking branding, labels, or instructions. The cans appear to be made of a lightweight aluminum alloy, resistant to decay, such as rust or acids. The cans seem to have a somewhat high air pressure within itself, so it is highly recommended to take caution if one desires to experiment on the pure substance. The substance inside is an opaque, greenish-gray liquid with an unusually thin and watery consistency, almost appearing diluted. However, when sprayed or applied, it reacts immediately with the surface or substance, frothing up into a thick, foamy layer that rapidly expands in volume. This process resembles a soap-like lather, however the foam quickly dissipates once wiped away. The foam is often white when sprayed, however some substances/surfaces often cause the foam to change color.

It seems to have no extremely harmful effect if ingested or applied onto oneself. In fact, Corrosion Repellent is one of the only known treatments against Ferrumasthenes Automatametallosis, more commonly and simply known as Ironlung Disease.

ANARKM-12.title: ANARKM-12
ANARKM-12.description:
ANARKM-12 is an automatic assault rifle designed by Agorean arms company Guzzler-Boon in 2012 within Level 92. The ANARKM-12's design is a slightly modified version of the renowned AKM meant to be an alternative to Auriellen/M.E.G Kalthoff Rifles, used by many offshoots of Agoreas and anarchists.

The ANARKM-12 was designed with the intention of being effective in most combat settings a freedom fighter would find his/herself in. It has also slight deviations from the AKM due to resource constraints within the Backrooms.

What is unique to the ANARKM-12, is that it isn't purely used for its destructive power, it is a symbol. A symbol of anti-state sentiment used by mainly Agoreas and political dissidents from other GOIs, and nothing gets that message acros clearer than having a preference of using the ANARKM-12 associated with Agorean revolutionaries over the corporate associated Kalthoff Rifles.

The ANARKM-12 is effective in close quarters combat due to its high damage per shot and powerful recoil. A well-placed shot can quickly neutralize an opponent. Its 7.62mm ammo deals a lot of damage, which is great for enemies up-close. However, the recoil can make it harder to control during rapid fire, especially in tight spaces. If that happens, it naturally is fitted with a bayonet to fight comfortably without ammunition. Overall, the ANARKM-12 excels in indoor levels and tight spaces which categorizes most levels.

The ANARKM-12 is scattered all around The Backrooms in large amounts and can be seen sold by unaffiliated wanderers or other affiliated organizations as scrap. Due to this, the rifle is easy to pick up. The rifle is very much soldier-proof due to its durability, requiring minimal maintenance and can endure rust, snow, mud, as well as temperature extremes from boiling to freezing.
ANARKM-12.lead:
ANARKM-12

A baseline ANARKM-12 laying atop an accessory crate.

Object 61.title: Object 61
Object 61.description:
Everbulbs seem to resemble vintage light bulbs, with the opacity of its glass and amount of light emitted varying based on the location it is found in. Everbulbs seem to take the appearance of any other light bulb within the level. If the light bulbs in a level, or region of a level have a certain set of features, say, the glass encasing the mechanisms for the light bulb are more opaque, and the light bulbs are bigger than most, then the Everbulbs that may be found in the level will have the same physical features. Everbulbs are often rarer in levels where the light bulbs are higher quality and well-maintained. This effectively makes Everbulbs with clearer glass, that emit more light, a more rare and prized possession or collectible across The Backrooms.

These Everbulbs are, as previously somewhat mentioned, very rare to come across in The Backrooms, and for good reason. The main, most glaring detail of these Everbulbs, are that they never go out. After many tests from M.E.G., it has been concluded that even if the filament inside is destroyed, every piece that formerly used to compose the filament, will still proceed to glow, unless a chemical reaction or process turns the metal composing the filament into a new substance. The crushed, powdered form of Everbulbs are also in market, but is sold far less, as it is somewhat more useless than an actual Everbulb.

Everbulbs are also found rarely in some anomalous levels. If an Everbulb is found in an extremely chaotic or unstable level, the light inside and glass encasing the bulb will both develop swirly patterns, with the inside of the bulb seemingly having a swirly sort of glittery fog or liquid inside. Due to the rarity of the form of these Everbulbs, experimentation of them by M.E.G. has been forbidden. There are many names for these Everbulbs, including "Everswirls", "Galaxy Bulbs", "Whirlpool Bulbs", and the name formally recognized by M.E.G. and most other large organizations, "Gyric Everbulbs" (derived from the Greek word "Gyro", meaning rotation, or spiral.) . These Everbulbs are far more rare than the other aforementioned Everbulbs, and are highly valued by collectors and wanderers alike.

Object 18.title: Object 18
Object 18.description:
Object 18, colloquially called "Kaboom-Boxes", appear as silver or gray colored, retro boom-boxes commonly found in the 80s or 90s era. Similar to a regular boom-box, the amount of buttons and controls vary between individuals. While dials and knobs can appear on Kaboom-Boxes, they are non-functional and should be ignored. All Kaboom-Boxes are impervious to almost all known forms of damage, Because of this their density appears to be higher than normal. Higher density means carrying them with you can be troublesome in fast paced escape. On the back side of all Kaboom-Boxes found to date there is a logo for a company called "Death Metal Studios". It is currently unknown who or what exactly this organization is. Their only goal so far seems to be to produce Kaboom-Boxes and spread them about the Backrooms for wanderers to use. Their method of transporting their product is unknown. Investigation into their whereabouts and intentions is currently ongoing.

Despite the chance for multiple buttons to appear on a Kaboom-Box, they all have the same function. When any button is pressed, the Kaboom-Box will activate and emit a loud tone between 50 Hz and 10,000 Hz and at a volume between 80 and 200 decibels. Due to the wide range of possible noises, effects vary from minor hearing loss to death. Via unknown methods, the user is unaffected by the noise emitted from the Kaboom-Box. After one use the Kaboom-Box will go into a "cool down period" lasting approximately 4 hours, using it again within this timeframe will result in a weak squeal and the Kaboom-Box breaking, becoming unusable. Examination of a broken Kaboom-Box has revealed that there are traces of Whisper Shrooms inside and some form of dynamo or electrical generator linked to a striker. Cassette tapes can be found around the Backrooms and inserted into the Kaboom-Box, when activated after inserting a cassette tape, it will play a recording of what sounds like a radio broadcast instead of its usual effects. The broadcast changes between instances of the cassette tape, but all feature the same DJ named "Brian". These broadcasts often contain irrelevant or inconsistent information, such as weather reports, news, and possible entities in your area. While this information is usually false, exactly 53 seconds and 98 seconds into the cassette tape, it will cut to static and a deep monotone voice will start to speak. This voice is unnamed and gives similar information as Brian, but with more accuracy, such as the locations of useful items nearby or the locations of exits in your current level. It is unknown when this broadcast was recorded, or where it is or was based. It is unadvised to seek out the creators of this broadcast, as there is no information as to whether they are humans or entities.

Amazing Tape.title: Amazing Tape
Amazing Tape.description:
Amazing TapeA Tezo's Art Supplies product

Description
Object 21 is the collective identity of spools of adhesive tape. Released in the early 1980s by Tezo's Art Supplies, Amazing Tape can be found in any level. There is a correlation to the type of level and how likely it is for Object 21 to appear, with urban and indoor levels much more likely to have the item. Offices show a extreme likelihood to have Object 21, not matter the level's properties.

Amazing Tape comes in one of two packaged forms. The first one is without any packaging at all. These collections only go up to 4 rolls of the product in any sort of arrangement. The second package is a clear plastic container. This plastic container has the Tezo's logo printed on top of it, and is kept shut by two plastic embeds. After pulling it open, wanderers will be able to access 8 uniquely designed spools of Amazing Tape. No matter how many is taken from the box, the next person who sees it, will see it full of tape. This visual oddity has been proven to be done 10 seconds after a piece of tape is removed from the box. When this happens. the tape will be the same as the one removed from it.

Each level which Object 21 was discovered in has 10 different types. The design of the object does not need to match with the level around it either, so the printed design of the tape is varied and inconsistent. These 10 styles tend to be referred to as "Level ### Pack", with the numbers representing the level tied to the tape. There is no reward for collecting all the styles.

The only other sightings of Object 21 have been within the level itself. In some cases, Amazing Tape acts similar to other strong tapes, with it appearing on drains or other cracks in walls as a sealant. Other times, the tape is used to add some visual difference to the level, with large and elaborate tape art being reported in places such as Level 4. Smaller ones have been claimed, but there has been no physical documentation saying otherwise.

Adhesive Properties
The extremely powerful adhesive properties of Amazing Tape makes it extremely durable to damaging, thus, its used in sealants as mentioned above. Yet, as a result of the anomalously adhesive abilities, using Amazing Tape is impossible. No one has shown the ability to use the tape, rendering it completely useless.

Snippet from "All about that tape" \\ irc-tezosarts-theme

Sigmaballs

Object 63.title: Object 63
Object 63.description:
Mimic Gloves are leather work gloves lined with a mechanical exoskeleton, made of an unknown metallic alloy that exhibits great resistance to physical and chemical degradation. The metal components have been recorded to withstand many forms of corrosion, high temperatures, and powerful acids such as Aqua Regia. Despite appearing aged and worn, the exoskeletons show no signs of structural weakness. The exoskeletal design of the gloves is eerily skeletal, featuring thin, overlapping plates that mimic finger bones, a reinforced knuckle guard, and delicate yet unnervingly intricate internal mechanisms. The inner lining is interconnected with a dense array of actuators, pressure-sensitive parts, and micro-mechanical joints reminiscent of high-quality prosthetic hands. These mechanisms are far more intricate and advanced than most other pieces of technology found in The Backrooms. The leather exterior of the gloves remains unnaturally pristine, regardless of its surroundings. Dirt, dust, and grime never accumulate on the material, leading researchers to suspect an unknown organic or self-cleaning process. However, no part of the glove appears to be biologically active, nor has it been observed to move on its own without external influence. An unexplainable property of these gloves is the sheer resilience of them. The metal, as mentioned before, is already incredibly durable. However, the durability of the leather should also not go unnoticed, as this leather is incredibly heat resistant, being able to withstand temperatures of around 2,500°C, and is infamously difficult to cut or slice. These properties are entirely unexplainable, as the leather on these glove have been examined to show that they indeed are genetically the exact same as any other sample of buffalo leather obtained in The Frontrooms.

The most prominent and dangerous property of the Mimic Gloves are its ability to amputate and tear hands and flesh. If one were to insert their hand into a Mimic Glove, the exoskeleton of the glove would immediately tighten around the victim's hand. If no measures are taken to remove the glove 2-3 minutes after wearing, the glove will proceed to become impossible to remove, as the leather hardens to a degree in which power tools fail to cut or physically harm the leather. As the glove continues to tighten, circulation is completely cut off from the hand. The affected tissue quickly begins to wither due to oxygen deprivation, and within an hour, necrosis sets in. The result is a full self-amputation, with the hand detaching and falling off, with the amputated hand being entirely decomposed within one to two hours. Researchers have concluded that the gloves "digest" organic material, as flesh and bone break down at an abnormally fast rate compared to natural conditions. The reason for this biological breakdown remains unknown. While the gloves have no apparent need for sustenance, they appear to consume organic matter with no visible purpose. It is not entirely known if these gloves necessarily need to eat flesh. After many experiments by researchers of a joint experiment by The Scrap Barons (more formally known as S.A.R.I.B.), and M.E.G., it has been concluded that either, the glove enters a state of hibernation, or the glove was never alive. All 3 hypotheses are entirely possible with research taken during this series of experiments. It could be possible that the glove enters a form of "hibernation", as the glove has not been observed to move at all except during its meals, and there is no determining if this glove has the capacity to "die". It may also be plausible that again, the glove was never alive, and its "need" for flesh is simply a coincidence, being that the components within the glove simply have an unexplainable property of breaking down dead material faster than in ideal conditions, and the suffocation mechanics are simply a malfunction.

Object 499.title: Object 499
Object 499.description:
✴ DESCRIPTION

Kharthic Energy (alternatively "Kharthiact"), is a form of Backrooms-native energy transmitted through psychic noise. Similarly to sound or heat, Kharthic Energy travels in the form of waves, harboring a range of different frequencies which alter its general effects. Kharthic Energy does not require a transmission medium to move between areas and is thus able to travel through a vacuum. Additionally, Kharthic Energy cannot pass through matter. Due to such properties, it is believed that certain entities in the Backrooms may have the unique ability to sense the reception of Kharthiact, though this remains unconfirmed.

Kharthiact is measured in RY (Reyennes), named after Swedish physicist Fredrik Reyenne who was the first to document and record his observations regarding Kharthic Energy. The Frontrooms is the only known example of a location possessing an absolute zero of Kharthic Energy; measured at 0RY. Most levels hover in the range of 2-3RY. A lethal dose of Kharthiact depends on the individual, though exposure to ~550RY for approximately 60 seconds may be considered fatal for most. It is important to note that Reyennes are measured on a logarithmic scale, meaning that every integer increase in Reyennes is equivalent to double the amount of Kharthic Energy.

  ✴ INTERACTION

One of the most notable properties of Kharthic Energy is its exceptional efficiency in producing and refracting light. High amounts of Kharthic Energy tend to produce a faint prismatic glare as a result of the refraction. Attempts have been made to utilize this property as a form of renewable and reliable lighting, although the drawbacks of Kharthic Energy's effects on the human mind have made this endeavor aimless.

Human exposure to high levels of Kharthic Energy tends to cause heightened anxiety, paranoia, and tension in the individual's mind. While the exact reasons behind why are unknown, these symptoms link to a considerable increase in adrenaline production alongside random neuron firings in the occipital and parietal lobe of the afflicted individual. These can cause extreme levels of stress, disorientation, and severe triggers in the fight-or-flight response. Non-mental symptoms such as twitching and inconsistent flashing in the eyes may also be prevalent depending on the received frequencies of Kharthiact.

Kharthic Energy is able to interfere with electrical currents, posing as a large obstacle to understanding information regarding its origins. Stray waves of high-frequency Kharthiact are prone to flipping or meddling with bits in computer memory. As a result, using drones or remote-operated machines to mitigate the effects of Kharthic Energy during research is essentially infeasible, as they are highly susceptible to corruption. On rare occasions, retrieved data from these corrupted devices can be decoded.

  ✴ FREQUENCIES

The rate of psychic noise vibration, alongside other factors, can determine the specific frequency of a Kharthic Energy wave. As for sound, Kharthic Energy uses the Hz system for determining frequency.

  ✴ PRODUCTION

Kharthic Energy is typically formed as a result of psychic noise collisions, in which psychic radiation collides with itself and is converted into Kharthiact as a product. This process tends to generate minimal amounts, however, as collisions are relatively unlikely due to their atomic size. Since Tulpae are manifestations produced from the Achospic Plane, they are also known to form Kharthic Energy when interacting with objects as a converted form of sound energy. As a result, sounds made by Tulpae are softer than usual.

The core of Level 500 is additionally known to generate immense amounts of Kharthic Energy. While studies on the core are limited due to its corrupting effects on retrieved information, it is assumed to operate in a manner similar to stars from the Frontrooms. Particles of psychic radiation — specifically those with a greater influence against matter — collect in the core's center, where fusion occurs and generates Kharthic Energy. Research on Level 500 has concluded that the level likely distributes excess Kharthic Energy to nearby levels; levels with a shorter "distance" to itself such as Fragmentation receive far more in quantity than those which are "further".

  ✴ DOMESTIC USAGE

Small canisters storing Kharthic Energy can rarely be found across the Backrooms, with appearance rates having no preference for any particular levels (aside from those which do not harbor objects). The only known means of containing Kharthic Energy is through certain patterns of supplying electricity to solid zirconium metal; when holding an electrical charge, zirconium can absorb waves of Kharthic Energy on its surface. When electrocuted once more with a pulse rather than a held charge, Kharthic Energy can be released from the metal.

Since November 4th, 2018, the Republic of Aurielle has begun testing the effects of high-frequency Kharthic Energy as a means of improving the performance of soldiers in combat. Due to its properties of producing adrenaline in the brain, dosages of Kharthic Energy supplied to soldiers' nervous systems are believed to be able to boost their physical capabilities. Consequently, however, obstacles have been encountered regarding the unfavorable side effects of paranoia and anxiety as a result.

  ✴ BIOLOGICAL USAGE

The use of Kharthic Energy in the biological processes of entities is speculative at best, as no confirmed instances of life using it to their advantage are currently known. It is theorized that Lamenting Sinners emit low frequencies of Kharthic Energy as a form of self-defense against wanderers. While variant B of such entity emits the energy in minimal levels with an average of 20-30RY, variants A and C are capable of causing greater effects of mental turmoil and hallucinations.

Ocelot 1.title: Ocelot 1
Ocelot 1.description:
JagP79 Ocelot 1

Ocelots performing live-fire exercises on the flatlands of The Chain

The Ocelot 1 is a main battle tank and assault gun designed and developed by The System Offensive Mechanics in 1979 and produced in 1983. Developed in an era where entity onslaughts and armed conflicts saw a significant increase, the Ocelot's design focused on having both the capability to effectively deal with smaller targets, waves, fortifications, and armored vehicles, which have become increasingly common throughout the years. Since its initial production in The Warzone, it has served as a vital resource exported and operated by the Republic of Aurielle's armed forces. Original plans for the Ocelot 1 were heavily inspired by the mechanical aspects of the T-72's design.

The design for the Ocelot was developed from the failed Project 71 that intended to replace the dated ZIS-5 anti-aircraft mounts. There are four operational Ocelot versions: the 1A1M, 1A2-J, 1A2-S, and 1A3, though older variations are kept as emergency units. Unlike many Frontroom tanks, the Ocelot does not have a common armament among all its different variations, ranging from a smoothbore 125mm cannon to 4 23x115mm KwK automatic belt-fed cannons. These are operated by the SOM Ohotnik 3 fire control system, laser rangefinders, and a The System-licensed Auriellen day-night thermal imaging camera. The tank is powered by a V12 SE1 dual water-cooled diesel engines.

The Ocelot 1 entered service in 1983, where it was deployed to assist forces in The Warzone; from then on, it has participated in various other conflicts, mainly by Aurielle. The Ocelot is currently only operated by The System and the Republic of Aurielle; however, various other tank designs, mainly the Cascadan YQ-9 Lǎong, are inspired by it.

Talking Heads.title: Talking Heads
Talking Heads.description:
Talking Heads are a collection of 5 salt statues installed across various Backrooms levels in 1998. The artist, Helena "Jill" Keina confirmed them each as her own, and has stated a lack of interest in producing more of Object 42. When wanderers step into a 5 meter radius of Object 42, they activate and start to speak. The topics the statues are able to speak on are perfectly emblamatic of the human it was based on, and only acquire new information through interacting. None of them have shown distaste with their situation, and have jokingly adivsed wanderers who spoke to them to also turn into a Talking Head. Object 42 is unaffected by rain and any other environmental damage, retaining perfect shape and quality.

How the statues were placed and given the ability to speak remain unanswered. But when Helena was asked for an explanation on the artwork, she provided an artist statement. This artist statement has been inscribed below, and all other attempts to reach out to her about the work have failed.

Object 101.title: Object 101
Object 101.description:
The One That Fuels All
The Voidstone is not one, but all. It is everything The Backrooms has known. It is everything. Every resource, every thought. Made from this. The Void is just its liquid form.

Once, there was nothing but a stone. It expanded, grew, transformed. It recolored itself into everything you see. It changed materials, soft, paper, rock. The levels are connected by it. Built from it. It is inescapable.

The stone is untouched. Buried in the deepest pits of the void, impossible to reach. It bends and twists, something new every time. It can destroy or create at a whim. It will never be seen.

Somewhere out there, the heart exists. Fueling it all. Branching off in hundreds of directions, to every level ever discovered and more. It is a web of imagination. A web of existence. It never sleeps, nor awakens.

And so, it may return to a state above all, as it is God. A god that controls all, sees all, and thinks all. It is otherworldly.
It is not a thing. It is a one.

The Heart
The heart is out there, somewhere. The mother of The Backrooms. It has no shape, nor sense. It is simply there, watching, observing. It changes and it warps into what it wants. Sometimes it makes a new level, sometimes it removes one.

The heart is incomprehensible. Only it can truly understand. It has power beyond an imagination. It is one, yet all. From the day it began, to the day it ends. It is control, order, life. A god.

The power it holds is unlike any other. It thinks what is real. Reality is merely its thoughts. Unimaginable yet so simple. This being is both existent and non-existent. It defies all logic. Everything was built around, is, it.

It is like The Holystone, the one that created The Frontrooms, but it didn't stop at one. It kept going. One, two, three, then a thousand more. When it ran out, it went the other way.

The Veins
The Veins connect every level to one another. They are the sole reason why one can no-clip here in the first place. A wanderer can no-clip to another level through them, but they do not all lead to one another.

They are like wires powering a city. Without them, it would be dark, alone. They help but also hurt. They can take one from a safe level to a dangerous one, or vice versa.

These veins are like those in the human body, yet instead of organs, there are levels. Infinite expanses of dismay and fear, or happiness and tranquility. They are like red strings on a corkboard.

"Sometimes, your greatest enemies turn out to be your friend. They leave you in a situation, and fix it."

It is all, including you, . You are it, it is you. Yet you will never understand.

> Open Author & Licensing Information

> Close Author & Licensing Information

Object 101-A.title: Object 101-A
Object 101-A.description:
The Seed Of  Birth
The universe started as nothing, no space, no time, no life. Nothing could describe it, since it itself was nothing. The universe was a blank slate, a vessel waiting to be filled, a sea without any water. Its potential could be anything, but it was absolutely nothing. It was a starting point, a Seed, that had the ability to become everything. The Seed, the Holystone. Two names for the same thing.

The Tree
Then, the Seed began to sprout. A stem began to move upwards through a spaceless dimension, branches dividing and deriving farther and farther from the universe's origin, roots taking more energy, more potential from the emptiness that laid below.

The Tree reached to the sun, and the sun moved higher and higher into the abyss. The Seed did not take its energy from the emptiness that surrounded itself, it took its energy from its own potential. And soon, branches became intertwined with their own galaxies, stars forming near the tree, each object taking their energy from the Tree's potential. The Tree was not the universe, it was what the universe would be. It was a nurturer, it created the future. The Tree was a 3D version of the universe's full potential, slowly growing, allowing room for more territory to be explored. The Tree was its own destiny.
The Cracks
But, as the Tree grew taller and taller, the stars and the galaxies wanted more and more. The Tree was what the universe was destined to become, but the universe wanted a different destiny. The universe wanted to become even greater than it was meant to be. And, as the Tree's power began to dwindle, Cracks began to form in its branches. The Tree had reached its highest point, but universe still wanted to reach the limitless sun, the universe was powered not by the Holystone, but by the Voidstone. It did not care if it would become a mindless hell, it did not care if it would become a purgatory. It was blinded by power.

And in a fit of rage, the universe broke through the Cracks, pushing through the boundaries, creating this world you no-clipped through. Craving more, it pushed further. First Level 0, then Level 1, and then a thousand more.

And, as the Holystone began to rot, the Tree desperately trying to hide its many scars, we must face a grave possibility that will soon become true.

At some point, the Tree will break. And then, there is no more home. This, this place the universe gifted cursed us with, this is our new home. This is our new world.

And who knows if this new world will break as well.

And who knows if this new world will break as well.

As the Tree begins to fall,
It will bring the end of it all.

> Open Author & Licensing Information

> Close Author & Licensing Information

Object 1.title: Object 1
Object 1.description:
Object 1, more commonly known as "Almond Water," is a naturally flavored beverage commonly found within numerous levels of the backrooms. It is the most abundant source of hydration and is a crucial item for avoiding critical dehydration while traveling. Almond Water has a sweet almond/vanilla taste mixed with purified rose water. It is found in several forms of bottled containment including plastic bottles, metal cans, glass wine bottles, or even cartons.  Almond Water has also occurred to be dispensed in water fountains, or found concentrated in bodies of water. Why, and how Almond Water became so abundant within the Backrooms remains a mystery.

How exactly instances of Almond Water manifest in levels of the Backrooms is unknown. They can be discovered, but no occurrences of them appearing at their location (through unknown occasions) have ever been observed or recorded. Almond Water, according to current understanding, will appear in a location where no observers can see it manifest. This phenomenon can occur within a room currently not occupied, or behind certain objects in rooms already occupied by wanderers. There doesn't seem to be any motive as to where Almond Water will spawn and in all circumstances, will appear completely at random.

Almond Water, when consumed, has been observed to cure minor symptoms of illness, along with minor improvements in well-being. It has been shown to completely neutralize effects inflicted by hostile entities. Wanderers have also reported feeling more focused after drinking Almond Water. With these benefits, Almond Water provides a slight source of nutritional value. Whether or not these effects are caused simply from the nutrition of the water, or from something more anomalous is of only subordinate importance. Other effects, more rare and from special instances of almond water, may arise depending on the type of drink.

Almond Water will, over time, begin to expire if not kept fresh. When expired, mold will start to grow within the liquid appearing in colors such as white, blue, or green. Wanderers describe its expiration as the smell of rotten milk and cheese. Drinking expired Almond Water is discouraged and can be poisonous, comparable to the dangers of drinking Cashew Water.  Symptoms include but are not limited to weakness, fatigue, dementia, insomnia, internal bleeding, and aneurysms. Deaths of wanderers have been reported from ingesting Almond Water left expired for long periods.

Almond Water is one of only several items to exist both in the Frontrooms and The Backrooms. Almond Water, within the Frontrooms, is a European-inspired beverage produced by Victoria's Kitchen. The recipe for Almond Water originated in France from the family who owns the said company who would, later on, decide to start selling the drink.

In the Backrooms, Almond Water appears, in most cases, within exact copies of the bottles produced by Victoria's Kitchen in the Frontrooms. In certain instances, however, a different look such as coloring on the bottles can be found. These bottles of Almond Water are rare, and such instances of them are most commonly reported in Level 4, though the bottles have also been reported in various other levels that include Almond Water. The supposed effects of the uncommon variant differ from the ordinary ones, as it can increase muscle mass and stamina. Bottles of these trade for high-valued items and the different colors have been observed to have different effects on a wanderer, and as of current only three of these colors have ever been found.

Almond Water, because of its value, is widely utilized for trade within different groups, and in some cases, has been standardized as a type of economic currency. Different flavors, colors, containers will have different face value based on its supposed rarity.

Object 66.title: Object 66
Object 66.description: Abyssal Soup , sometimes referred to as Niandra's First Choice, is a runny, odorless, pitch black liquid which can be obtained from various levels. Abyssal Soup has several properties depending on the person drinking it. Each individual will have a different experience when consuming Abyssal Soup, and effects are dependent on personality. Object 66 has a melting point of -103°C and a boiling point of 147°C. Its taste is similar to that of chicken noodle soup.

Object 35.title: Object 35
Object 35.description:
The Diary

The Diary is stored in a climate-controlled vault, within a hermetically sealed, transparent display case made of polymer. Only certain researchers are allowed to interact with The Diary. All individuals interacting with The Diary must undergo a psychological evaluation before and after exposure.

Under no circumstances are written, printed, or digital materials to be brought within 10 meters of The Diary without prior authorization. Audio or video recordings of The Diary are strictly prohibited, as transcription attempts have shown signs of memetic corruption.

In the event of unintended exposure, affected individuals are to be immediately quarantined and monitored for signs of progressive linguistic deterioration or auditory hallucination. If symptoms persist for longer than 48 hours, therapy is to be administered.

Description

The Diary is a red leather-bound journal, measuring 23cm x 15cm, with no visible publisher markings. Carbon dating of the leather suggests a creation date between 1920 and 1940, though the ink and paper do not match any known historical or regional production methods. Despite appearing aged, the book shows no signs of decay, and pages are resistant to tearing, water, and fire.

Upon opening The Diary, subjects report immediate sensory anomalies. These include:

* A voice "reading" along in the subject’s native language.
* Peripheral motion (shadows, figures) appearing.
* Onset of synesthesia, typically involving written language appearing as colors.

Text within The Diary varies per subject and reading session. Content ranges from incoherent symbols to fully formed journals, often describing the reader’s own thoughts or memories. Several test subjects have reported the journal predicting events up to 48 hours in advance with impeccable accuracy.

Subjects exposed to The Diary for more than 30 minutes begin to experience paranoia and dissociation. After 60 minutes, comprehension of any written language—whether native or learned—begins to deteriorate. In 12% of cases, subjects lost the ability to interpret spoken language entirely, a condition currently irreversible.

Addendum TD-1: Recovery Log

The Diary was recovered from an attic in Seileta, Aurielle, in 2014, following reports of four local residents missing under mysterious circumstances. The object was found sealed inside a lead-lined box, accompanied by a note written in a language similar to Aurielleno, roughly translated as:

"Seal it with silence, and never let it speak again. Words are wounds."

Addendum TD-2: Incident Log A
Date: 2015-09-17
Subject: Dr. Marcus Llewyn

Dr. Llewyn insisted on conducting a solo study of The Diary’s pages. After 43 minutes of exposure, surveillance footage recorded him speaking in a language unidentifiable by linguists. He then attempted to write on the walls. Security intervened; Dr. Llewyn resisted and was sedated.

Post-incident, Dr. Llewyn showed complete aphasia and an inability to recognize any alphabet, including those he had previously learned. Neuroimaging indicated abnormal activity in certain areas of the brain.

Status: Permanently reassigned to long-term psychological care. Exposure time limits reduced to 15 minutes for all researchers.

Addendum TD-3: Audio Log (Expunged)

A recorded voice was captured during a test involving a prisoner. Despite no speakers or audio devices present in the room, a faint voice is heard saying:

"To read is to be rewritten."

Following this event, all tests involving automated audio equipment were discontinued.

Addendum TD-4: Degradation Log

On 2020-03-01, Dr. Halvorsen attempted a passive scan of The Diary using a non-invasive neural interface. No physical interaction with the object occurred. However, within 90 seconds, the data feed began returning corrupted data. This was followed by an observable shift in The Diary's file itself on The Database.

Note from Lead Researcher Halvorsen:
We believe The Diary is not a book in the traditional sense. It is a linguistic parasite, a predatory structure that uses cognition itself as a host. Every attempt to categorize it—whether verbal, written, or computational—results in structural degradation of the framework used to contain it. This document is likely already compromised by its presence.

We are no longer asking "What is The Diary?" We are asking: what language did it come from—and who still speaks it?

All further testing has been suspended indefinitely.

[This version has been stabilized. Do not reopen the original file. Do not think in prose. Do not give it your words.]

Researcher’s Journal – Dr. Caleb Myles
Internal Archive – Restricted Log (TD-A)

I requested access to The Diary’s file for the second time today. First time I read it, I didn’t really feel anything. Just a weird book that made other people go nuts. All of that felt... theatrical.

But something’s been weird since then. A phrase in the margins that I don’t remember typing: “What if it’s remembering you, too?” It wasn’t in the official version. I checked. No one else sees it.

I see it.

Researcher’s Journal – Dr. Caleb Myles
Internal Archive – Restricted Log (TD-A)

They told me not to read the earlier revisions. I did anyway. There's a version from 2018, badly corrupted—half-gibberish. But between the lines, there were shapes.

Not drawings. Just letters that looked like they wanted to be images. Like they were trying to form something bigger than themselves.

It’s 3 a.m. I’ve been rereading the same paragraph for an hour, and I could swear the page layout changes when I blink.

My memory is slipping. I forgot my own name badge this morning. I forgot the name of the Director, and I’ve worked here five years.

I didn’t forget the phrase, though.
“To read is to be rewritten.”

Researcher’s Journal – Dr. Caleb Myles
Internal Archive – Restricted Log (TD-A)

They quarantined Dr. Mendelson today. She tore a copy of her own research notes into strips and tried to swallow them. She kept repeating something about "clean pages."

I haven’t told anyone that I found new entries in my notebook.

They’re in my handwriting.

Researcher’s Journal – Dr. Caleb Myles
Internal Archive – Restricted Log (TD-A)

No one else sees it.

Not the margin phrases. Not the small glyphs appearing on the backs of their badges. Not the sound—a soft, constant whisper beneath fluorescent lights. A voice without breath.

I asked Dr. Halvorsen what it says. He just blinked at me and said, “What voice?”

I think they’re infected. I think I’m the only one who knows it.

Researcher’s Journal – Dr. Caleb Myles
Internal Archive – Restricted Log (TD-A)

Retrieved from shredded notebook, reconstructed by researchers.

"The words aren't in the book. The book is in the words. And now the words are in me. Stop writing. Stop reading. Or it will know your name, too."

Postscript:
Dr. Caleb Myles was found unresponsive in his office, surrounded by hundreds of torn paper strips, all blank. No traces of ink. No visible content.

His notebook was recovered from his desk drawer. It was completely empty—except for one phrase, written in small block letters on the final page:

"I HAVE ACCEPTED YOUR ENTRY."

> OPEN FILE

> OPEN FILE

> OPEN FILE

> the margin speaks

> the book listens

> did it write you too?

> END

> BADSUM   > BADSUM   Written by Lunon. hi hi hi

Object 8.title: Object 8
Object 8.description:
A pair of unused Magic Door Knobs in the custody of the M.E.G.

Object 8, also known as Magic Door Knobs, are very rare objects found in all levels of the Backrooms, regardless of their nature. They resemble standard brass passage door knobs but lack the parts necessary to mechanically function as one (such as the chassis or spindle), consisting only the knob and backplate. A thin groove secures the backplate to the knob's end, allowing the knob to spin freely within the backplate. The knobs are spherical in shape and are smooth by the touch, devoid of any engraved design on their surfaces.

When a Magic Door Knob is placed on a wall that is at least 36 inches by 80 inches large with adequate flatness, it will adhere to the wall immediately and be secured in place. Once fixed to a wall, they cannot be physically removed without inflicting enough damage to render them unusable. When the installed Magic Door Knob is turned 90 degrees, regardless of direction, it will lock in place and cannot be rotated. Once pulled, it instantly transforms a 36"x80" portion of the wall into a functional door.

The “door” created from Magic Door Knobs will feature a door frame that matches the material and three brass hinges on one side.  Which side the hinges are located is dependent on the Magic Door Knob’s location on the entire wall (See Testing/Additional Information). Both the frame and the hinges are impossible to be removed or destroyed.

What lies behind doors created by Magic Door Knobs is determined by their location and will lead to any level in the Backrooms containing a suitable wall. What levels link to these doors does not appear to follow a pattern, but it will be the same for all doors within a specific radius.  Once a living entity has fully entered the door and is no longer being observed, the door will automatically close shut and vanish along with the Magic Door Knob, returning the portion of the wall to its original state.

Despite their seeming commonality, Magic Door Knobs are never found on doors, although they can be found in a variety of other places; some have even been spotted lying in the center of empty, open areas. There is insufficient data to indicate that they occur in greater abundance in specific levels. The prevalent belief that there are more Magic Door Knobs on levels containing doors is somewhat illogical, as they cannot be found on doors. It is unknown whether they manifest similarly to Almond Water, or there is a finite amount of them within the backrooms.

Object 39.title: Object 39
Object 39.description:
Object 39 is a white cube known as Transportation Cube by the M.E.G. It has a physical size of 1 dm3 and due to its compounds made of 78% Iron (Fe) and 22% Tungsten (W), the cube weighs roughly around 5 kg. It's theorized that its origins are from Level 12, due to the cube's similarity in color and anomalous properties. Its use is to transport any humans or objects to a targeted level via one's command.

The cube ha a special "parent level" phenomenon, the cube can't be brought out of the level it was discovered; however, there is only one recorded way possible. The cube must be put into a airtight seal (Vacuum Chamber) and exit the level without usage of the object. If one doesn't follow these procedures then the object will disappear without a trace. It also should be noted once one uses the object, the individual(s) will experience a mild headache for around 30 minutes after the usage of the object as a sort of side-effect. The object also has a cooldown period where the individual(s) that were transported/used the object can't use the object for a week after the final usage.

Tests done by M.E.G. and Backroom Colonists, both physically and biologically, have shown that:
* The object is resistant to any damage known to common materials. (corrosion, rusting, melting, etc.)
* The object can only be used by humans, other entities cannot use the object.
* The object cannot be replicated or disassembled in any way.
* The object requires fuel to use. (read Testing Log O-39-1)

Because of the usability of the object being very powerful, its distribution has been cut by a significant amount. This is to avoid any bad actors (especially enemies of such groups) to have the object in stock and use them to ambush key levels in The Backrooms. Powerful and research groups have an abundance of the object, which has led to the rarity of encounters with the object. Wanderers who encounter the object have been said to either use it themselves or sell it for survival resources in secret deals.

NOTICE:

THIS SECTION OF THIS PAGE CONTAINS CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.

In accordance with unilateral confidentiality agreements, this page is classified; access is strictly prohibited without proper clearance. The Database has requested the release of this section to be de-classified, please wait for further response.

> Kauer Research Organization Section

> Close Kauer Research Organization Section

> Kauer Research Organization Section
The Kauer Research Organization has conducted many research in this particular object. And they have theorized that how the object works is by the fuel that is given and quantum tunneling. The fuel acts as the catalyst for the process of transportation and used to modify certain atoms in the individual(s) to transport them to their designated level. As quantum tunneling had happened with the invasion of the Partygoers, it's believed that it's the only explanation that their research has given.

M.E.G. Database Login Form
