Nothing ever happens.

Welcome, !

Object 34.title: Object 34
Object 34.description:
---- Description
----

Object 34, or Elevators, are one of the most sought after and useful objects throughout the Backrooms. Instances of Object 34 manifest as traditional office elevators, though instead of traveling between floors of a building, Elevators travel between levels. Upon entering an elevator, one will notice a series of four to fifty buttons, each marked with a number. Each instance of Object 34 only serves a finite number of levels which is labeled by the buttons. Once one pushes a button for their desired level, the doors of the Elevator will close, before it ascends or descends to the requested level depending on one's position in the Backrooms at the current moment.

The appearance of an Elevator remains starkly similar no matter which level it is located in. Each Elevator possesses metallic gray walls, tiled flooring, and overhead ceiling lighting. Though variations on appearance exist, they are mostly confined to the rooftop lighting, floor design, and the position of the buttons. Elevator shafts also retain the same appearance, though their placement varies widely. While Elevator shafts are usually found in typical locations such as interior hallways, Elevators can be found in more unorthodox places, such as within trees or hills, behind doors or windows, and much more.

Most Elevators travel along fixed routes and remain in the same place. As a result, many colonies and outposts are centered around
Elevators, using them for all means such as exploring and documenting new levels and collecting resources. Within many Elevators, any colonies and outposts that are accessible from the Elevator are marked via various means such as tape and marker over the buttons.

The quantity of Elevators on levels decreases as the number grows higher. For instance, Level 0 possesses the most Elevators, while Level 997 only contains two elevators. Anomalous levels are completely inaccessible via traditional Elevators and can only be accessed using Random-Chance Elevators which is a risky move. This, strangely, includes Level 999, which contains no Elevators whatsoever and is treated similar to an anomalous level within the Elevator system.

Currently, the longest Elevator route is between Level 0 and Level 989, avoiding the nine penultimate levels completely.

---- Variations
----

---- Random Chance Elevators
----

Random Chance Elevators are an unstable variant of Elevators that can be used to access every known level in the Backrooms, though comes at a serious price. Within Random Chance Elevators, the buttons within regular Elevators are replaced by a singular button at the center. Random Chance Elevators takes one to a completely random level of the Backrooms with each level having an equal chance. Unlike regular Elevators, Random Chance Elevators can be found in random and often anomalous places, such as atop buildings, clipped into walls or other objects, and in deep underground/underwater locations. Upon the arrival of a Random Chance Elevator on any level, obstacles are moved to different positions, and a shaft is formed that resembles conventional Elevator shafts. Once a Random Chance Elevator leaves, the shaft will vanish, and the area reverts to its original state.

Due to the instability of Random Chance Elevators and risk of being sent to a dangerous level, it is advised for one to avoid using Random Chance Elevators unless it is absolutely necessary.

---- Keypad Elevators
----

Keypad Elevators are similar to typical Elevators, though the button panel is replaced by a keypad with a typical QWERTY keyboard format. Similar to Random Chance Elevators, Keypad Elevators are believed to be able to access any level. However, each level is connected to a code that one must enter to travel to their desired level. Many available codes within Keypad Elevators are still unknown, though a few have been mapped out as of now:

*"0" sends one to Level 0
*"FR33 P@RK!NG" sends one to Level 1.
*"H0TEL" sends one to Level 5.
*"B00!" sends one to Level 9.31.
*"WALSPT" (We All Love Some Pool Time) sends one to Level 37.
*"G3N3RATING" sends one to Level 66.
*"THE 0TH3R H0TEL" sends one to Level 187.
*"THE 0TH3R 0TH3R H0TEL" sends one to Level 188.
*"26032009" sends one to Absolute Infinity Of Unknown.
*"1000" sends one to Level 398.
*"N30N!" sends one to Level 399.
*"THE W@TERP@RK" sends one to Level 969.
*"THE TOWER" sends one to Level 989.
*"TICK3TS PL3@SE" sends one to Level 36.

Many other codes probably exist, though they remain undiscovered. It is unknown whether levels that have unique, specific methods of entry can be accessed through Keypad Elevators. Due to the semi-unpredictable nature of Keypad Elevators, attempting to discover new codes is risky. Unless one is thoroughly prepared for new levels, it is advised to only use the codes listed above.

---- Hazard: Malfunctions
----

Malfunctions are deadly properties specific to Object 34 manifesting as events occurring as a result of anomalous electrical/structural failure. The mechanism behind Malfunctions are currently unknown, with the only known information being that Malfunctions happen once every 1000 rides using Elevators, or a 0.1% chance.

The following is a list of documented Malfunction events from survivors or witnesses.

Report 1:
Date: 1/5/16
----
Summary: Two wanderers enter an Elevator located on Level 19. After a few minutes of riding, the Elevator catches on fire. Luckily, it happened to stop near Level 238, and one wanderer was able to leave via a crowbar at his disposal. Once the door is pried open, the other wanderer managed to escape. Both wanderers survived, albeit with serious injuries.
----
Cause: Most likely a spark resulting from an incorrectly placed wire.
----
Notes: First encounter with a Malfunction overall.

Report 2:
Date: 11/14/18
----
Summary: A group of five wanderers enter an Elevator on Level 20. 5 minutes into the ride, the Elevator suddenly falls down and hits another Elevator traveling on the same route containing three wanderers. All three wanderers on the bottom Elevator perish on impact, along with two from the top Elevator. The three remaining wanderers use brute force to burst open the doors, revealing them floating within The Void, though right on top of Level 36. After a coordinated leap of faith, two wanderers manage to land on Level 36, while one misses. The remaining two wanderers miraculously only had minor injuries, while the last one has not been recovered.
----
Cause: Overcapacity
----
Notes: None.

Report 3:
Date: 7/24/20
----
Summary: After B.J's interview, a security camera was placed within a few elevators serving Level 399 and Level 499. One of those cameras captured an exploration team from the 399 Megacolony boarding the Elevator and pressing the button for Level 205. 35 minutes into the ride, a fire starts in the southeast corner, igniting an oxygen tank the team was carrying. Immediately, the tank explodes, bursting the Elevator and its occupants into flames. All six members of the team died due to the incident.
----
Cause: Same as Report 1.
----
Notes: This began the start of a measure to include security cameras within Elevators to track the frequency of Malfunction events.

Report 4:
Date: 9/7/21
----
Summary: After the above report, more security cameras were installed on Elevators. Another camera captured a wanderer boarding an Elevator from Level 622, bound for Level 324. An hour into the ride, the entire Elevator becomes shrouded in darkness and comes to a halt. As the camera's night vision functionality turns on, it captures the wanderer using a flashlight to find something to break down the Elevator's doors with. Upon finding none, the wanderer proceeds to using his body weight to break the doors down. After a few tries, he succeeds, and he rolls onto the grounds of Level 500.
----
Cause: Faulty lighting.
----
Notes: This is currently the least deadly Malfunction event documented.

Even though Malfunction events are highly unlikely, it is advised for one to watch for any flaws or errors if one utilizes an Elevator. Since Malfunctions are more likely to happen within Random Chance Elevators, this further encourages the use of those types of Elevators only when absolutely necessary.

---- Do's and Dont's
----

---- Do's
----

*Do a thorough investigation of each Elevator before using one, unless it is an urgent situation

*Come prepared for anything if you choose to take an Elevator.

*Mark any viable outposts or safe resources above the buttons.

---- Dont's
----

*Assume nothing will happen.

*Attempt to tamper with any feature of the Elevator (button mashing also counts).

*Use Random Chance Elevators frequently.

Object 51.title: Object 51
Object 51.description:
Classified as Object 51, Enigmatic Sheds are an infrequently encountered object within various outdoor or open levels across The Backrooms, particularly those that mimic suburban environments. Instances of the object, as their name implies, appear as an average-sized bicycle shed. Enigmatic Sheds are constructed from a rough, plastic-like material that is resilient to damage and wear, as attempts to destroy them have proven fruitless. Each encounter shows that instances of Enigmatic Sheds have pale gray walls, while the ceiling and doors are constructed with a darker shade of gray. Additionally, occurrences of the object seem to be fixed in place by unknown means.

As mentioned before, the general location of Enigmatic Sheds is suburban-like environments. They typically manifest behind houses, facing away from the outer walls. However, in some cases, the positioning of the objects seems unusual. For instance, Enigmatic Sheds may appear clipped into objects, atop roofs, or seemingly levitating. Despite this, the characteristics of the objects always remain consistent. Although relatively uncommon, reports of the object have seemingly increased in frequency over a recent period. Encounters initially started in Level 9 and its sublevels, though the presence of the objects has spread throughout numerous other levels.

While appearing harmless at first glance, Enigmatic Sheds hold various anomalous properties. Examples of such include ringing in the ears while in proximity, sudden drowsiness or fatigue, a heat sensation in one's eyes, and a feeling of paranoia or anxiety. However, many of these are either rare or very mild, though one must still be cautious while approaching an instance of this object.

----

Object 43.title: Object 43
Object 43.description:
The object 43, commonly referred to as Emotional Glass or Funny Cup, is a glass capable of producing a liquid that is identified as Liquid Emotion. This liquid provides the drinker with an emotion corresponding to the colour of the liquid.

In general, Liquid Emotion generally has a colour which correlates to the emotion that it provides. For example -

*Yellow = Happiness

*Blue = Sadness

*Red = Anger

Liquid Emotion is produced any time a person picks up the glass.  The glass will automatically produce the emotion which corresponds to the current emotional state of the person holding the glass. For example, if an angry person picks up the glass, it will produce a red Liquid Emotion, which represents rage/ anger.

Liquid Emotion cannot be poured into any other glass or cup (except an empty Emotional Glass), or it will evaporate instantly. It is also dangerous to mix different kinds of Liquid Emotion - when combined, it turns black, has the texture of ink, and smells like dirty water. Upon consumption, the person will pass out and become either Almond Water or Liquid Pain, or Carpet Fluid, for reasons yet unknown.

Most Emotional Glass have been located in Class 1, 2, 3, or other habitable levels. Because it can provide so many useful effects, it is often used as one of the main forms of trade in some habitable levels. It is essentially infinite—every time you drink it and then pick it up again, it automatically refills.

In some cases, explorers have traded items or other valuables to get someone to refill their Emotional Glass with emotions such as calmness, happiness, or sleepiness. This is because the Emotional Glass can be useful in the Backrooms, mainly in levels where it is essentially impossible to sleep or feel calm. These emotions can be almost impossible to experience until someone uses an Emotional Glass, which then opens the door to access calmness or happiness.

One level where the Emotional Glass has been used in abundance is Level 622, which has many shops that sell emotions considered exotic or difficult to feel.

The A.A.E.G. has researched Emotional Glass to help determine the source of its unusual properties. What they found is that structurally speaking, there is nothing out of the ordinary about Emotional Glass in terms of the physical structure. This means that Emotional Glass can be really difficult to identify or locate because it looks like all other Cups.

Emotional Glass could potentially be used as a weapon too. One way would be to purposely make Black Liquid Emotion and give it to someone to drink. You could also just give someone an Emotional Glass filled with a negative emotion like rage, or fear. Emotional Glass could be used for manipulation by giving a person an emotion like happiness or calm and fooling or controlling them to believe everything is okay.

The Emotional Glass has, in addition to other uses, also been used to support several refugees to Level 622, as it has properties that alter (and quicken) anyone's emotional state. The Emotional Glass has also been used to alter the confidence of explorers before they travel into hazardous levels. However, certain illegal shops in The Backrooms sell it for smuggling. There is an illegal form of Liquid Emotion, called black water, made by mixing many different kinds of Liquid Emotion before selling it. Some people use black water for suicide and others use the chemical to harm others, both actions being made easier due to the extreme danger posed by the black water.

In addition,it leaves a substantial stain on clothes and creates noxious odours that can cause queasiness for some and considerable discomfort for others. There are still efforts to track individuals who traffic this kind of liquid, but their identification is extremely difficult, as any trafficker seldom appears on the same level twice.

Typically, the consequences of the Emotional Glass come with certain disastrous results, such as severe addiction. In this case, in Level 622, drinking the Emotional Glass is usually restricted to one per day.

Many people have been reported to develop a very serious addiction to it, to the extent that they will panic if they are unable to drink Liquid Emotion after only a few hours. To quit using this addiction is almost impossible, especially when Emotional Glass is rather easy to find (while it is somewhat difficult to identify one outright). After just a little searching you will frequently find at least one in under an hour.

As a result of the addictive properties of the Emotional Glass, all third-party sales have been outlawed; and the only legitimate form of consumption is from the A.A.E.G. official shops.

Object 300.title: Object 300
Object 300.description: You are viewing top-classified information. Please input your password to verify if your rank is high enough to view this page.
Object 300.lead: You are viewing top-classified information. Please input your password to verify if your rank is high enough to view this page.

Object 46.title: Object 46
Object 46.description:
Sensitive Candies are multi-colored sweets coated in layers of white sugar, widely used throughout The Backrooms. They are easily portable, usually weighing less than 5 grams.

Their distinctive minty smell distinguishes them from other candies with similar appearances. Upon consuming them, all of one's senses may be strengthened for an hour, including vision and hearing.

Object 12.title: Object 12
Object 12.description:
Liquid Pain, designated Object 12, is a viscous substance found in most levels of the Backrooms. It is prominently known for its corrosive properties, capable of altering the chemical composition of various materials and objects. It has a striking red hue, and emits toxic crimson fumes. Samples of Liquid Pain are often found in nondescript containers falsely labeled as other objects or substances.

Further testing has determined that Liquid Pain has a pH of around 1-to-2, giving it properties similar to hydrochloric acid; it is able to dissolve soft substances such as flesh, styrofoam, and paper. Due to this, Liquid Pain is often stored in metallic or otherwise thick containers. Despite this, it is still able to corrode most materials with ease, save for most steels, Kevlar, and various metals.

When extensively exposed to air—commonly around three-to-four days—it will harden and turn into a resin-like substance; the substance does not pose immediate danger, yet has the chance to spontaneously combust, rupture, or otherwise explode. Due to this property, wanderers have weaponized hardened Liquid Pain in the form of bullets, arrow tips, and other rudimentary projectiles.

Object 99.title: Object 99
Object 99.description:
custody (temporarily).|321x321px]]
Object 99, aka "Destabilizer", is an anomalous object resembling a modified pink & red toy walkie talkie presumably designed for children. Despite this object's small size and unassuming appearance, it is one of the most useful and mysterious items in the Backrooms. It is unknown whether Object 99 is singular, as walkie talkies usually come in pairs, but there have never been reports of contact with Object 99 in overlapping time periods, so it is impossible to say for sure.

On its front, the device has 6 black ovular buttons in a semicircle pattern around a green number display, and 3 other buttons below them. At the bottom, a speaker is present for relaying audio. An antenna and light are present at the top. The back side of the object has a small panel that can be removed to view its interior.

Though not visible from the outside, this object has undergone extreme internal circuitry modifications (including the removal of any identifiable power source), which are likely the cause of its anomalous properties (see Usage). The delicate internal machinery has so far dissuaded anyone from attempting to dissect it for fear of breaking it.

Object 200.title: Object 200
Object 200.description:
Niraloka Black, formerly known as Matte Black, was a unique type of black paint. It absorbed 100% of all light with no margin of error, creating a pure black surface. It is currently 'extinct', but was found in Level 9, Level 6, and Level 0.23, alongside a temporary manifestation as its home level. This temporary space was generated by The Cross, simultaneously creating the object.

|-| Veil Ink=

Veil Ink, also known as God Blood, was the primary core of Niraloka Black. Veil Ink consists of three main chemicals: Sodium Hydroxide (15.24% abundance), Bromine (21.53% abundance), and Liquid Pain (63.23%). This makes Veil Ink an extremely corrosive substance. This chemical has only been separated from Niraloka Black, as it has not been found elsewhere. Whilst it has been hypothesized that Veil Ink can become a solid, there was no definitive proof.

|-| Negative Charge Converter=

The Negative Charge Converter was an intangible barrier, separating the Negative Particle Ring and the main core of Veil Ink. When passing through the Negative Particle Ring, the light's positive charge was influenced and converted to a negative one. The Negative Charge Converter converts this negative charge back to a positive charge, causing that light to be suspended between the Negative Particle Ring and the Veil Ink, as both carry a negative charge. The Negative Particle Ring has a much stronger charge. As a result, the light was slowly pushed to the Veil Ink, where, through unknown means, the photon particle was destroyed. No light escapes once it has touched the paint. It was comprised of Sha-Particles.

|-| Negative Particle Ring=

The Negative Particle Ring was a ring composed of Zhe-Particles, the sole purpose of which was to use its negative charge to repel light within its barriers. It was intangible and anomalously had no charge on its exterior, allowing light to pass through freely. However, when light passes through and reaches the Negative Charge Converter, the Negative Particle Ring becomes negatively charged on the interior. These particles that make up the ring are hypothesized to be variations of Noble Gases, as they have no radioactivity.
Object 200.lead:
Notice
Object 200, known as Niraloka Black, has dematerialized, and no reports of it have come in. Therefore, it is safe to say that the object no longer exists, or in other words, is 'extinct'. This was in conjunction with the dematerialization of  Level 9.9. While reading this page, keep in mind that Object 200 no longer exists.

Object 14.title: Object 14
Object 14.description:
Object 14, otherwise known as "Paperstone," is a smooth white sedimentary stone found more noticeably in various aquatic levels across the Backrooms. It is well-known for its many strange properties. Paperstone is typically able to resist against extreme conditions like weight, radiation, or pressure due to its high compressive strength and radiation resistance. However, due to its brittleness, it can be effortlessly broken with minimal strength. Paperstone seems to become stronger and more resistant throughout its formation and in differential erosion.

Another well-known property of Paperstone is its bioluminescence and radioluminescence, emitting a soft blue glow when exposed to high radiation or darkness. The radioluminescence occurs because of phosphorus in the object's composition, which makes it glow under radiation. Meanwhile, the bioluminescence is caused by fungi and microorganisms that grow on Paperstone during its formation and after. This especially happens due to the wet environment that surrounds most instances of the object.

Paperstone is often considered a mixture of various rocks, such as sandstones like itacolumite, salt rocks, calcite, bioluminescent rocks, diorite, and others. However, the full composition of Paperstone remains a mystery.

For various reasons, Paperstone's composition and properties can be effortlessly changed, resulting in objects like Papersap, a molten and liquid variation found in Level 865. Another example is Paperstone Glass, a thin and crystallized instance found underwater or in tundras. Paperstone Glass is a sought-after material in aquatic levels as an easier-to-find replacement for regular glass. It is unknown how Paperstone Grass is exactly formed. It is known that Paperstone undergoes a process of crystallization under exceedingly cold temperatures; this process is important to its formation, seeing as Paperstone Glass is crystallized. However, tests did not result in a substance like Paperstone Glass.

The name Paperstone comes from the rock's paper-white coloration, its malleability, and how effortlessly it breaks with human strength.

IDI/I.D.I.O.T..title: IDI/I.D.I.O.T.
IDI/I.D.I.O.T..description:
V.C.U. Transmission Log - August 27, 2024

"Is something wrong, Thatcher?"

"No- well, yeah. Did you see the news?"

"What news?"

"The recent IDI sighting in Base Alpha."

"Oh... yeah. What about it?"

"What do you mean 'what about it?' That thing is class 5 in intelligence! If it hacked into Base Alpha, it more than likely knows about us."

"Right. So what do you suggest we do? I can see why even then, we'd likely have to create some sort of counter to IDI."

"Exactly."

"...Huh? You mean- ah. I get it now. An antivirus, right?"

"Precisely. We can't risk IDI, or any type of malware in fact, compromising one of our devices. The data we hold is very important. So I think our next project could be dedicated to creating something that can protect us from any virus attacks."

"All of this because someone's little weapon got out of hand..."

"Yep. We should get to work. We can't waste time on this when we also have to solve out The Void."

"Oh, I can tell you, right now, this is the smallest of our problems."

I.D.I.O.T Antivirus

The notification display that appears when the I.D.I.O.T. malware is activated.

Overview
The IDI Defense, Inspection and Observation Tool (I.D.I.O.T.) is an antivirus created by the V.C.U. designed to specifically target IDI. The antivirus was developed in late 2024 to counter the increased sightings of IDI that emerged during that time period. The I.D.I.O.T. antivirus can be adjusted by the organization using it to match its specific security concerns or proper communication mediums.

The antivirus works by automatically resetting the device's file data upon detecting any anomalous activity from an external source via heuristics, such as USBs or anomalies. Upon encountering a fatal error or a system breach, it will by default perform a factory reset and force the device into shutdown for 12 hours, effectively blocking out IDI. The device can be turned on manually if it is optimized to allow it.

Additionally, it has been programmed to recognize IDI's patterns. Upon activation, it will notify the security field according to how it has been adjusted by the organization in possession of the device and warn the owner of the device of possible entity presence.
Flaws
The I.D.I.O.T. Antivirus is not completely foolproof. If the device with the IDIOT antivirus is outdated, old, or broken, IDI can still manifest within it. Additionally, it is important to note that if IDI is manifested physically, the IDIOT antivirus is ineffective, and it is possible for IDI to memorize the patterns of the antivirus due to its intelligence.

As a result, it is recommended to not completely rely on the I.D.I.O.T. Antivirus to take safety measures against IDI. If the antivirus is triggered, it is best recommended to turn off the device manually if its use is currently unnecessary and follow the basic survival means documented by The Database. When in levels filled with devices or technology, make sure that all devices are in good condition and up-to-date to make the best use of the antivirus.

And most importantly...

Object 400.title: Object 400
Object 400.description:
"Work Juice" appears as a standard, 12-ounce, white, ceramic mug filled with a dark, opaque liquid resembling black coffee. The mug is plain, with no observable markings or branding, and the liquid maintains a constant temperature of exactly 165°F (74°C). The liquid does not evaporate and refills itself automatically whenever it is empty.

Found exclusively within Level 4, this object's primary anomalous property activates upon consumption. The liquid, despite its appearance, smells faintly of almonds and vanilla, a key indicator of its unique properties, though it lacks the texture and specific taste of typical Almond Water.

Upon drinking "Work Juice" the consumer experiences an immediate and profound surge of mental clarity and energy. Fatigue, hunger, and dehydration are temporarily nullified for a period of about eight hours. Subjects report feeling highly motivated and focused, capable of performing complex tasks without error or distraction. This effect makes the object highly sought after by various Groups for sustained exploration and resource gathering.

However, the effects are not without consequence. After the eight-hour period, the consumer experiences a significant decline in their condition, resulting in a state of extreme weariness and heightened unease. This period of recovery can last up to twelve hours, leaving the individual susceptible to the dangers of the Backrooms, like entities or phenomena. Using "Work Juice" too frequently (less than 24 hours between uses) can lead to a build-up of negative effects on the user's mental state, potentially causing confusion and difficulty in perceiving their surroundings accurately.

Due to its potent, albeit temporary, utility, the M.E.G. has cataloged "Work Juice" for emergency use only. Protocols dictate that any user must be monitored and quarantined during the post-effect recovery period. The self-refilling nature of the mug means it is a stable, infinite resource, though caution is highly advised due to the significant strain its after-effects impose.

Object 80.title: Object 80
Object 80.description:
Object 80, also known as "Holiday Baubles", are round glass ornaments about the size of an orange, each containing a small pine sprig and visible "snow." The interior surface is covered with tiny clear beads resembling crushed ice, and they have metal caps with hanging loops. Only the orange size has been recorded. The glass seems harder than normal glass but remains breakable. The bauble emits waves of cool air in a small radius of up to 5 cm.

These baubles are very rare in the Backrooms. It is unknown how they arrived here. They may randomly manifest within the Backrooms. No records exist showing how or where they manifest, so their origin remains a mystery. It is theorized they appear in areas where no wanderers are present, similar to Almond Water.

The main detail about these baubles is that they can send a person to one of several different Backrooms levels. After many tests by the M.E.G., it was concluded they transport wanderers to different Christmas-based levels.

Several attempts to break the baubles were reported to the Kauer Research Group. Breaking a bauble causes the snow inside to melt immediately and releases a mixture of gases and a liquid form of Burkharts Gas. Because the gas is exposed to warmer temperatures, it condenses into liquid. Other gases released when the bauble breaks produce the cooling effects, but these gases could not be captured, so the exact mechanism is unknown, though it is believed to result from constant chemical reactions produced by mixing gases inside the bauble.

If a holiday bauble suddenly produces more snow than usual and begins to glow, it is charged and ready to use. A wanderer may store it for later use or rip out the silver hanging loop. Doing so, will cause the bauble to glow so brightly, that it will temporarily blind nearby wanderers, however, this blinding has never been recorded to cause injury or harm, to wanderers. After the brief blinding, the glow rapidly dims and the wanderer, will finds themselves in a Christmas-based level, such as Level 941.

After sending someone to a level, the bauble will be completely empty, howevfer, the pine sprig will still remain inside the bauble. After a while, snow will slowly beginto fall from the top, in the empty bauble, gradually refilling and "charging" the bauble. When it is about one-third full, it will glow again and be ready. Usually it takes a week to refill, but the charging process takes longer if ambient temperatures are below 10°C. Each degree below 10°C delays charging by one day.

The bauble is hard to find and is typically located in dark corners or inconvenient spots. They very rarely they appear in obvious places. The floor is usually frosted around them due to the cooling waves they produce. These cooling waves reach -5°C, making prolonged contact dangerous. It is advised to keep a bauble in a backpack rather than in pockets or held in hand.

Object 65.title: Object 65
Object 65.description:
}

      Utility: }/5

      }

      }

      Risk: }/5

  }
    Properties:
    ≫ } ≪

    Description
Velocity-Neon is a liquid with anomalous properties.

Velocity-Neon has speed-altering properties, accelerating other objects at a heavily increased rate when they come into contact with this substance. A smoother motion between molecules was observed when the substance was poured on an object. It was concluded that the anomalous properties of energy in the Backrooms influenced this liquid heavily. Leading to Velocity-Neon altering the way molecules interacted around it and thus increase the speed of an object dramatically.

Additionally, Velocity-Neon gives off light. The light itself is neon colored, being either bright blue, purple, or pink.  Strangely, the light emitted from Velocity-Neon never flickers or fades, making it a viable light source.

Other liquids or gases have been known to interact oddly with Velocity-Neon. If there is an electrical current going through the liquid, it will be amplified, creating bright flashes and loud noises. Electricity will arc through the air, striking the objects or living beings that are the closest to this electrical hazard. All items or beings struck by this object will travel much faster than usual before the effects of the lightning set in and kill or damage the item or being in question.

If any liquid comes into contact with Velocity-Neon, the solution created would be diluted, dulling the effects of the serum. Usage
There are multiple ways for one to use Velocity-Neon listed below.
 Ingesting: Consuming Velocity-Neon haphazardly is not recommended. In the event that the wanderer does consume Velocity-Neon in unregulated quantities, the wanderer's movements be quickened, along with other bodily systems. However, it has been noted that the structure of a wanderer's body is not strong enough to endure such strenuous effects. The wanderer's heart will pump at an increasingly alarming pace, leading to blackouts and, in some cases, death. The thinking processes of a wanderer are not affected, as Velocity-Neon does not usually affect mental or psychological properties. Injection: If done by a professional, this method may have benefits for the wanderer. Injecting drops of Velocity-Neon into the wanderer's body over time is encouraged, allowing the wanderer to adapt to the effects of the liquid. The safest place to administer it is near the arms, as far away from vital organs as possible. Injecting it near vital organs may cause heart failure or the ribs crushing themselves against the lungs.   If the procedure is a success, the wanderer will move quicker and be able to outrun certain entities with ease. The wanderer's metabolism and respiration rate may quicken as well. There is only one downside to this—every 4 months or so, the wanderer will experience immense pain, which is followed by loss of consciousness for about 2 days. It is believed to be the result of the overloading of bodily processes by the Velocity-Neon.

Rubbing/Coating Objects: Rubbing or coating objects with Velocity-Neon is the safest way to use the liquid. The object will move faster for a long period of time. Since Velocity-Neon evaporates at an extremely sluggish rate, the effect it has on the object will linger for a long period of time. This is usually done by wanderers on the base of their footwear to slightly increase their running speed.

Coating Velocity Neon on objects can also help to push or move them with less force.

    Locations
    Velocity-Neon is commonly found leaking from appliances that are turned on for a very long span of time. Examples of such appliances are the lights of Level 0 and the electricity stations of Level 3.  Research conducted has found that the buildup and long-term release of electricity or energy causes a shift in the molecular structure of normal liquid. This leads to the formation of Velocity-Neon. However, it is still unknown why the liquid gives off a neon-colored light. Since energy can be found almost anywhere in the Backrooms, especially in strange forms, Velocity-Neon is able to be found nearly everywhere. Exceptions are given to levels that are devoid of color, life, or energy, such as the Void.

    Variants
    There is only one variation of Velocity-Neon, which is Velocity-Void. This variant of Velocity-Neon is extremely dangerous and not to be used by wanderers in any form. Wanderers should refrain from making contact with the liquid due to its dangerous properties.
    Velocity-Void is a liquid formed when an extreme amount of energy is output onto normal liquids such as water or Carpet Fluid. The type of energy that usually triggers the formation of Velocity-Void must be powerful and, in most cases, reality-bending.  As such, most levels with cracks or fissures leading to The Void have pools of Velocity-Void forming in them.
    In summary, Velocity-Void is an enhanced version of Velocity-Neon. However, Velocity-Void increases the speed of a moving object so fast that the wanderer or object it is used on glitches through many parts of the Backrooms, all in the span of a second. This is known as Quantum Fissuring. This term is used to describe the object or wanderer teleporting through many levels of the Backrooms at once. It is speculated that the object or wanderer would accelerate at an uncontrollable pace, leading to the fabric of reality, or in this case unreality, to be torn. As such, levels that are hotspots for Velocity-Void pool formation are extremely unstable.
     Attached Information
    A log of an experiment conducted by the M.E.G. has been pinned down below.

Cadaver Asada.title: Cadaver Asada
Cadaver Asada.description: Haiiii , I'm Melody Belle Evenstar, better known as the Fleshmonger and one of Caius Taggert's Co-Host entourage. When I heard of this ultimate cookbook thing, I thought "no way they're gonna make a manifesto of recipes without inviting me", so... WELCOME BACK TO COOKIN' WITH MELODY! This is a dish called "Cadaver Asada", one of my personal favourites, and is featured as a tool in a poison-based murder in Caius's recent original musical, Fiddlesticks!

Object 27.title: Object 27
Object 27.description:
Mixologist's Delight is an alcoholic cocktail commonly found in bars on Level 610 and the stores and vending machines of Level 33, although the object can be found in other levels, usually on unorthodox places. The cocktail itself is usually found on a martini glass; however, it can also be found on other glasses. The color of the cocktail heavily varies, though it is typically a light turquoise, normally found with a lemon or orange garnish. Wanderers report that the drink tastes sweet and refreshing, though with a citrus and bitter aftertaste. The martini glass itself is hard to break, as dropping the glass will not cause it to shatter; however, the martini glass can be broken through a harder force. The cocktail does not have a recipe and can only be found naturally within the Backrooms, though the cocktail is moderately common within the mentioned levels and rare within other levels.

Mixologist's Delight is normally expired, though Mixologist's Delight that is not expired can be useful to wanderers, therefore; unexpired Mixologist's Delight is extremely valuable. Drinking expired Mixologist's Delight will initially cause symptoms such as explosive diarrhea, vomiting, severe food poisoning, and moderate headaches. Continuous consumption of expired Mixologist's Delight will cause the previous symptoms to become worse, as well as causing new symptoms such as a high fever, sepsis, and possibly turn wanderers into a Wretch. To tell if Mixologist's Delight is expired, an expired Mixologist's Delight cocktail will be darker than the unexpired variants. The cocktail shows mild addictive qualities, though these addictive qualities are not strong enough to be considered "drug-like", instead being slightly higher than that of sugary sweets or standard Frontrooms snacks.

Object 9.title: Object 9
Object 9.description:
Object 9, more commonly referred to as Gate Carvers, are replicable electronic devices used to create apertures in ground, roof, and wall surfaces. They are used to create temporary or permanent gateways to other levels. Their effects can be deactivated by simply patching the opening. Where they lead can be manipulated via altering their programs, which primarily use the programming language Fortran, or rarely, C. Unmodified units require continuous external power, though the ones altered by wanderers may contain internal compartments where batteries could be placed. They are extremely rare, restricted to the most powerful Backrooms settlements. Thus, their existence was only known to the top leadership of M.E.G. and allied organizations until 2015.

Unlike most electronic devices in the Backrooms, Gate Carvers exhibit minimal human engineering. Traces of human contact (e.g., fingerprints, structural imperfections) were missing during its discovery in Level 1. Its appearance resembles LIDAR scanners but lacks essential components, such as scanning lenses, laser emitter indicators, and brand logos. Internally, however, it is filled with twisting, interlocking geometries, particulate inside sealed compartments, and floating cores. According to Backrooms Today and several intelligence sources hostile to M.E.G., the structural and material features were perfectly replicable using elements found in the Frontrooms and other substances within the Backrooms.

The earliest documented use of a Gate Carver was in 1999. Two years later, in 2001, its inner workings were first successfully reverse-engineered by an unnamed engineer in the Democratic Republic of the Survivalists. General information tied to the device has been deliberately declassified gradually, but the underlying physical principles remain unresolved. Additionally, the rationale pertaining to the Gate Carver’s proliferation remains tightly regulated and has been the root of several tensions among organizations in the past.

Object 52.title: Object 52
Object 52.description:
+++Object 52, also known as “Aurelia Suffocans,” “Auresuffo,” and the “Chill Weeds,” is a highly invasive twining vine with a decorative appearance. Its foliage grow with heart-shaped leaves characterized by a distinctive metallic, and brassy sheen caused by a reflective cuticular layer on the leaf epidermis. The plant grows groups of small, and pale yellow flowers. They emit a acrid odor of burnt sugar mixed with ozone.

+++The species appears to have fast vegetative growth, particularly in humid environments, where its height could grow up to 30 centimeters a day. Its flexible stems have strong thigmotropic behavior and allows them to wrap around nearby both natural and manmade structures. The plant can grow in a somewhat short time compared to other plant species, covering vast areas with its vines; thus its invasive plant classification.

+++Aurelia Suffocans reproduces through not only sexual but also vegetative propagation. The plant releases microscopic, and sharp structures. These barbed propagules function similarly to burrs, which stick onto entities fur or fabric, which aid distant dispersal. Additionally, its seeds have a heat-resistant outer cover, which protects them in the soil for up to ten years before germinating under suitable conditions.

+++Auresuffo has strong vegetative regeneration. Any chopped stem contains active meristematic tissue that can produce new roots upon contact with soil or moisture. Due to this, attempting to remove it only makes its spread faster instead of stopping it.

Object 22.title: Object 22
Object 22.description:
This document has been partially declassified as of 22/07/2011.

Object 22, also referred to as a 'cross', is a medium-sized device with three pronged legs. It has a tall, black body surrounded by dark steel plates. Nestled between these plates are small orange cylinders, acting as the fuel storage.  The Cross has shown the ability to manifest entities and destroy objects.  It is believed that the Cross manifested anomalous abilities after continued use. The Cross also opens "rifts", holes in levels that lead to The Void. It can be noted that multiple test participants have shown biological changes, specifically to the genome. Increased protein production is often a side effect of interacting with the device. The device is now more valuable than all of the combined assets and incomes of Bilbury, England.

The first iteration of the device was created by the M.E.G., before being seized by the Party Crashers. The second iteration was handheld and tested in the Ieptus Testing Institute; It was then transported to the Starclip Testing Institute to be repaired after the device was damaged. The third and final iteration of the device was developed in the Frestway Testing Institute, remaining operational until The Flicker. The Flicker causes the core to collapse, destroying itself.
Standing in front of the operational end of the device during activation is ill-advised, with internal bleeding and death almost guaranteed. Symptoms include:

* Strokes
* Seizures
* Internal bleeding
* Haemorrhage
* Cancer

A notable side effect of being hit with the device is teleportation, with some subjects being transported to The Void or The Broken.

The Cross has also shown the ability to "generate" objects, most notably Object 200 from Level 9.9. 9.9 was a notable temporary space, one of the first that remained stable for longer than a couple of seconds. During the generation of this space, numerous anonymous accounts bombarded BACK-NET with website-creation requests, spreading information about the level. It is unknown who the creators were and how they got this information. This information was often incorrect and had mistakes.

The Cross was designed in an effort to "force" a temporary space to open. It generates unstable geometry before firing a small aluminium pellet into the wall, effectively forcing a no-clip. While in the Void, the vessel utilises small thrusters to move around, avoiding stable areas. This forces a temporary space to open. A notable space is Level 9.9. It was similar in appearance to Level 9, and existed for just 7 days.

The Starclip Incident occurred on the 25th of December, 2010. JC walks into the Starclip Research Institute, towards the experimental weaponry branch. Numerous tests of the Cross were planned that day. JC enters the building, heading for the infirmary after falling down a flight of stairs. JC exits the infirmary at 14:53. JC immediately heads for Building B, entering at 14:56. Multiple devices are around, being tested live. JC puts on a yellow jumpsuit, preparing for the first test. JC enters a small section of Building B, the device sitting in the middle of the room. JC picks up a set of crucible tongs, using them to pick up the Core.

JC places the Core into the device, accidentally offsetting one of the metal plates. The Core begins powering up, going critical 2.3 minutes later. The remaining pieces of the device are destroyed, the core is generating a gravitational force similar to that of a small asteroid. JC exits floor 1, unaware of the core's criticality.

JC is found deceased on the second floor, blood pouring from his nose. The entirety of the experimental weaponry branch is evacuated, leaving only 15 casualties. The experimental weaponry branch is abandoned, alongside the entirety of the Starclip Institute.

PARTIAL TRANSCRIPT:

31/12/2010 - 15:23, The first test of The Cross begins. JC activates the device, the Roll begins to generate energy within the Core.

15:41, The cross finishes charging. Operative JC approaches the device, preparing it for activation.

15:43, JC accidentally interacts with the core.

15:44, rising heat signatures are detected in the core.

15:45, evacuation procedures begin. The core has been determined to be going critical.

15:48, JC is found deceased on the second floor.

15:51, the remaining Core goes supernova, collapsing the entire right wing of the institute.

Object 22.lead:
This document has been partially declassified as of 22/07/2011.

Object 22, also referred to as a 'cross', is a medium-sized device with three pronged legs. It has a tall, black body surrounded by dark steel plates. Nestled between these plates are small orange cylinders, acting as the fuel storage.  The Cross has shown the ability to manifest entities and destroy objects.  It is believed that the Cross manifested anomalous abilities after continued use. The Cross also opens "rifts", holes in levels that lead to The Void. It can be noted that multiple test participants have shown biological changes, specifically to the genome. Increased protein production is often a side effect of interacting with the device. The device is now more valuable than all of the combined assets and incomes of Bilbury, England.

The first iteration of the device was created by the M.E.G., before being seized by the Party Crashers. The second iteration was handheld and tested in the Ieptus Testing Institute; It was then transported to the Starclip Testing Institute to be repaired after the device was damaged. The third and final iteration of the device was developed in the Frestway Testing Institute, remaining operational until The Flicker. The Flicker causes the core to collapse, destroying itself.
Standing in front of the operational end of the device during activation is ill-advised, with internal bleeding and death almost guaranteed. Symptoms include:

* Strokes
* Seizures
* Internal bleeding
* Haemorrhage
* Cancer

A notable side effect of being hit with the device is teleportation, with some subjects being transported to The Void or The Broken.

The Cross has also shown the ability to "generate" objects, most notably Object 200 from Level 9.9. 9.9 was a notable temporary space, one of the first that remained stable for longer than a couple of seconds. During the generation of this space, numerous anonymous accounts bombarded BACK-NET with website-creation requests, spreading information about the level. It is unknown who the creators were and how they got this information. This information was often incorrect and had mistakes.

The Cross was designed in an effort to "force" a temporary space to open. It generates unstable geometry before firing a small aluminium pellet into the wall, effectively forcing a no-clip. While in the Void, the vessel utilises small thrusters to move around, avoiding stable areas. This forces a temporary space to open. A notable space is Level 9.9. It was similar in appearance to Level 9, and existed for just 7 days.

The Starclip Incident occurred on the 25th of December, 2010. JC walks into the Starclip Research Institute, towards the experimental weaponry branch. Numerous tests of the Cross were planned that day. JC enters the building, heading for the infirmary after falling down a flight of stairs. JC exits the infirmary at 14:53. JC immediately heads for Building B, entering at 14:56. Multiple devices are around, being tested live. JC puts on a yellow jumpsuit, preparing for the first test. JC enters a small section of Building B, the device sitting in the middle of the room. JC picks up a set of crucible tongs, using them to pick up the Core.

JC places the Core into the device, accidentally offsetting one of the metal plates. The Core begins powering up, going critical 2.3 minutes later. The remaining pieces of the device are destroyed, the core is generating a gravitational force similar to that of a small asteroid. JC exits floor 1, unaware of the core's criticality.

JC is found deceased on the second floor, blood pouring from his nose. The entirety of the experimental weaponry branch is evacuated, leaving only 15 casualties. The experimental weaponry branch is abandoned, alongside the entirety of the Starclip Institute.

PARTIAL TRANSCRIPT:

31/12/2010 - 15:23, The first test of The Cross begins. JC activates the device, the Roll begins to generate energy within the Core.

15:41, The cross finishes charging. Operative JC approaches the device, preparing it for activation.

15:43, JC accidentally interacts with the core.

15:44, rising heat signatures are detected in the core.

15:45, evacuation procedures begin. The core has been determined to be going critical.

15:48, JC is found deceased on the second floor.

Level 680.title: Level 680
Level 680.description:
Level 680 consists of an expansive city centered around a singular road presumably from the state of Russia within The Frontrooms. The city is covered in an eternal, thick layer of snow, and a constant mild snowstorm is prevalent throughout the level; some areas are snowed in more than others, although it is impossible to find a clear area. The average temperature of the level is -8°F, with recorded lows going as cold as -38°F.

The snowed-in town in permeated by a complex of roads and bridges of concrete, with some having collapsed into the snow. While technically traversable, it is unadvised, as serious slipping hazards arise from walking on the iced and snowed-over bridges.

Level 680's geometry can be described as cylindrical; if one walks down the central road within the level, they will continue onwards infinitely. Meanwhile, attempting to far enough away from the highway will result in one returning back to it. The level has been measured to be around 1.8 miles in width, and is theoretically infinite in length.
Level 680.lead: Level 680, known colloquially as 'Bezdna', is the 681st level of the Backrooms, and presumably an extension of Level 445.

entrances.text: In Level 445, traveling down a dirt road lined with trees will seamlessly transition the level into Level 680.
In Level 245, snowdrifts may randomly appear, noclipping into the snow will lead one to Level 680.

In Level 173, often in close proximity to exits to Level 445, patches of ice can be found floating. Interacting with them will cause a wanderer to black out and wake up on a bench in a Snegozaderzhateli.
In Level 690, snow may occasionally spawn atop a bridge, jumping into it will lead here.

In Level 258, staying inside a building at night may lead to the Gigakhrushchevka in Level 680. This transition is seamless and often goes unnoticed until the wanderer acknowledges the apparent size of the building, or when they leave the Gigakhrushchevka.

exits.text: Traveling deep inside the Gigakhrushchevka and finding a glitched mass of objects may lead to Level 404, alternatively leading to The Broken.
Level 684's entrances manifest as weathered paintings of, or advertisements for, image galleries or museums, hanging in the Gigakhrushchevka
Waiting too long at a Snegozaderzhateli will condemn you to waiting, forever.
Reminiscing of better times while walking the Gigakhrushchevka may segue into Level 193, Level 201 or Level 231.
Rarely, finding a subway entrance along the roads may lead to Level 95.
Extremely rarely, garage doors attached to the sides of Khrushchyovkas will lead to the garages of Level 158.

Object 67.title: Object 67
Object 67.description:
Object 67, also known as molten meat or spam, resembles a sludgy, frothy red fluid, sometimes mixed with a thin, clear yellow liquid assumed to be oil or fat. The substance is opaque and viscous, with a consistency similar to that of basaltic lava, and occurs mainly in industrial levels of the Backrooms such as Level 2, Level 3 or Level 105. The froth present in molten meat is uniquely and anomalously dark in colour. Despite its fairly unremarkable temperature, the substance is known to be extremely corrosive toward non-environmental surfaces (i.e., surfaces which are not inherently a part of its level's environment), and operatives have found it complicated to procure a sample of the object for research.

Molten meat exudes a gas, known as cruoric gas, which causes wanderers who inhale it to begin feeling exceptionally hungry, and makes their intestinal walls unable to absorb nutrients from almost any food which they may consume. If the wanderer has not eaten for a while, which is all too common for lone roamers of the Backrooms, they are liable to starve and perish. Additionally, the gas works its way into the wanderer's brain and forces them to subconsciously seek out the molten meat.

Upon setting eyes on molten meat, the wanderer will become infatuated with it, and experience a terrible urge to devour it so as to satisfy their unslakeable hunger. If cruoric gas has been allowed to sufficiently penetrate the brain, it can also induce a form of synesthesia, which disorients the wanderer and ensures the only thing which remains distinct to their senses is the meat. Patients who have inhaled the gas and survived have reported feeling an intense attachment to the molten meat when they first encountered it, declaring that they "could think of the meat, only the meat and nothing but the meat". (Such patients have explicitly described the object as being "meat", although they seem unable to provide an explanation for how they instinctively arrived at this notion.)

Unfortunately for those who consume it, molten meat is immensely toxic to almost all biological life, not to mention its corrosive properties. In addition, the cruoric gas it emits can shut down a wanderer's lungs if inhaled in large quantities, and anyone who has been exposed to it is advised to seek immediate medical assistance. No wanderer nor entity has ever been known to survive consumption of the object.
Object 67.lead:
I had been wandering the wretched halls of Level 1 for several days now. Some parking garage it is. There aren't even any cars in the Backrooms! Or, at least, they aren't a very convenient mode of transportation. But nonetheless, the Backrooms had still chosen an infinite, empty parking garage as one of its first levels. Ironic, isn't it? Imagine how much use a complex like this would have in the Frontrooms.

Why don't the two dimensions have a trade: Level 1 in exchange for a McDonald's or something? I'm getting really hungry. I still have some almond water left over, but I used up my supply of moth jelly three days ago, and the last of my sensitive candies ran out. If I don't find a base or an exit to another level soon, I'll starve to death.

That was what I was thinking when I saw it.

It was a bubbling mass of frothy redness and oils and fats.

but in that moment, that wasn't what I saw.

Object 54.title: Object 54
Object 54.description:
Object 54, commonly referred to as "Mailboxes", is a type of anomalous postal container. It is usually found in indoor or urban levels of the Backrooms.

Most instances of Object 54 manifest as simple metal containers mounted by the side of roads or other pathways, often near houses or doors, in building lobbies, or other locations where postal boxes would commonly be found in The Frontrooms. The appearance, condition, and style of Object 54 varies widely from instance to instance. Many Mailboxes appear to be small wooden, metal or plastic pouches mounted on posts made of wood or metal, similar to Frontrooms mailboxes. Others may appear as hollow spaces cut in small, ornate stone structures and sealed with small doors. A few appear as traditional large blue metal containers locked by key, similar to traditional postal containers placed in public spaces. On rare occasions, Object 54 may resemble large containers such as shipping boxes, safes or even vaults.

The primary anomalous property of Object 54 is its ability to transport objects placed inside it across levels.

The most common way (that is, the method used when the object takes the form of a normal mailbox) to transport items via Object 54 is to place the items inside, close the lid, and perform the action that traditionally indicates that mail is in the box for a postal worker to pick up, usually raising a flag on one side of the mailbox. Nothing will immediately happen, and opening the lid will reveal the items inside, unchanged. However, after a period of time which varies from mailbox to mailbox, but is typically 4-16 hours, a mechanical noise (commonly described as whirring sounds followed by a "ka-chunk") will emanate from inside the mailbox, and the flag will lower itself to its original position. Opening the mailbox afterward will reveal an empty interior. After 6-72 hours, depending on the mailbox, the items transported will arrive at their destination, deposited either in enclosed locations of other levels, in non-anomalous mailboxes, or in other instances of Object 54 (see Variants below for more information on destinations).

An experiment performed that involved "mailing" 5 active radio transmitters through an instance of Object 54 found in Level 4.3 revealed several insights into the travel undergone by "mailed" objects. As soon as the "mailing" sound was heard outside the mailbox, the radio transmitters ceased transmitting. However, over the next 36 hours, intermittent radio signals matching those of the "mailed" transmitters were detected at frequencies of 4 MHz, 1 MHz, and 154 MHz. It is suspected that the transmitters were "shipped through" Levels 4, 1, possibly the Hub, and Level 154. When the transmitters began broadcasting consistent signals again, it was found that they were transmitting on a frequency of 772 MHz. A subsequent expedition (thoroughly equipped with Almond Water and psi-resistant gear) into Level 772 found all 5 transmitters in a non-anomalous desk drawer.

BRC-TZ.title: BRC-TZ
BRC-TZ.description:
BRC-TZ

A BRC-TZ using an fixed stock with a JTND scope

The BRC-TZ BCR-TZ (Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium-Type Z; Aurielleno: Buseleum Cowensorcio Resorase-Tuwis Z) is a Auriellen 5.56×45mm NATO assault rifle developed in the mid 2000s by the state-owned small arms research and development manufacturer Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium based in Nereneavos.

The weapon was successfully trialed between 2006 and 2007 and approved for serial production in 2009 at Vallis Descando in Level 618. The BRC-TZ was intended to replace the HK33 as the standard service weapon of the Auriellen Ground Forces; however, it was considered too ambitious and expensive an undertaking, and thus it was ultimately decided that it would be placed in service alongside the other Frontrooms-derived weapons. In 2010, the first rifles were delivered, by which time orders for approximately 7,000 had been placed. From 2012, the BRC-TZ would be a weapon of choice and would become the de facto standard service rifle of the General Aerial Operations Command's air assault units.

Level 828.title: Level 828
Level 828.description:
Level 828, colloquially referred to as "The Fortune of Misfortune", resembles a series of infinitely expanding, interconnected chambers made of steel, which are roughly 30 m² each and have a hexagonal shape. These chambers have lights hanging from the ceiling on chains, and said lights grow dimmer the further one strays from the center. Each chamber is connected by short metallic tunnels that have no ores in them. All chambers within Level 828 are filled with various precious minerals, primarily Amethyst, Diamond, Lapis Lazuli, and Gold. Each of these minerals can be found under diverse circumstances and conditions.Other, lesser-known minerals have been reported to be present in the far reaches of Level 828, such as Ruby, Sapphire, and Bronze.

While the minerals and ores of Level 828 can be found in diverse locations and under various conditions, pattern analysis has identified specific areas where they are most commonly found. The analysis is listed below:

*Amethyst deposits are often found in isolated chambers where little to no other ores are present. This mineral is primarily present in the form of geodes and raw ores and can easily injure human limbs due to the sharpness of its form and materials.

*Diamonds appear scattered throughout all chambers in most cases. This noteworthy and valuable ore is found in the shape of a polished gem.

*Lapis Lazuli is an extremely rare mineral within Level 828, being difficult to find and appearing in minimal quantities. This mineral is found within the level's expanse either in the form of powder or jewelry, and has a high trading value due to its rarity not solely in Level 828 but also throughout the Wake.

*Gold is the most commonly found valuable ore in Level 828; it has a chance of appearing in every chamber and is typically found in high quantities. This ore is found in a multitude of forms, including but not limited to coins, rings, and ingots.

It is said that instances of Object 1 in the form of bottles can occasionally be found in the chambers of Level 828, though an official publication confirming these rumors is yet to be released.

The overall temperature of Level 828's chambers fluctuates between 30 and 50 degrees Celsius (86 and 122 degrees Fahrenheit); however, some chambers have natural heating systems that can increase the temperature to 162ºC, an exceptionally high temperature for the human body to handle.

Lastly, Level 828 has been deemed stable, with little to no environmentally altering phenomena such as Phenomenon 14. However, it is presumed that this renders exits few in number and difficult to locate.
Level 828.lead: Level 828 is the 829th ore of the Anomaly Mine.

entrances.text: Referencing the discovery method used by Enrico, no-clipping through a steel wall or surface in Level 242 (or on occasion Level 906 and Level 960) will lead to Level 828.

exits.text: No-clipping through the ground of an Empty Chamber may send one to Level 322.

Object 150.title: Object 150
Object 150.description: Object 150, referred to as Fiolgine, is an anomalous chemical element originally discovered within Level 680, designated with the chemical symbol Fo.
Object 150.lead: Object 150, referred to as Fiolgine, is an anomalous chemical element originally discovered within Level 680, designated with the chemical symbol Fo.

Object 56.title: Object 56
Object 56.description: Bottled Starlight‌, one of the rarest resources in the Backrooms, is a treasure that countless wanderers have dreamed of but cannot obtain. It is not an artificial creation, nor can it be replicated by any known means, and it only forms naturally under specific conditions, which means each ‌Bottled Starlight‌ is completely unique.

Object 155.title: Object 155
Object 155.description:
Butterflies in my sto[m]ach!
a work in descent

now i float numbly

My heartbeat messes up.
It messes up when I see him…
It races uncontrollably, I blush heavily!
Closing distance, thud, thud.
It's like I'm going to explode!
Butterflies in my stomach,
they fly freely.
Tingling my belly,
they seem to want to get out,
but just can't.
Until I'm face to face,
chest to chest.
His heat can be felt in my skin,
slowly pressing,
slowly accepting its embrace.
The butterflies free into the sky!
They dance brightly in a quiet bay,
spreading their wings.
The ceiling was no limit
to what they could reach!
As beautiful as his face.
I fade into a beautiful dark while holding tight.
So tight it marks my skin, with his relief.
I would be like this forever…
If it weren't fake.
I count them, aching.
They're bigger, round, purple.
They hurt, hurt, hurt!
His embrace left my body tainted.
Left me hurting, pained.
Where is it, where is it?
I search in my room, in the dark.

…

I found it.

…

Please work again.
Swallow, swallow, swallow.
Down my esophagus, into my veins.
It's integrated into my roots,
the aftertaste is a cascade.

in a sea of tears

cascading into a fluorescent red root
hung from a glass of champagne
the sizzle, the bubbles smell like methanol
sweetened crystals composed of ice and quartz
sacks inflate and take out and shine
as they become amber wood, water
flung the tears, salty dead
dead tubes of plastic cadmium
into a spiral, i am concrete
i nuzzled against in plea
please echo through the arms of the spider
traced the thorax and abdomen
the deep needles of skin
metallic notes reminiscent of the champagne
mingled with a chlorinated wine
down, the nexus tingled, the plastic bottle
d[a]nce, dance with me!
intertwine into a mix of limbs!
the legs of the spider long for you
wish i could section your tendons
yet I'll be happy with a carcass
drink, drink mindlessly!
overflow the lymphatic with fluid
why talk outside a window
when the castle of yours has been with you?

only
melt
into
the
sweet
sweet
aroma
of
the
champagne-tasting
chlorinated
ever
shifting
concrete-li[k]e
wine
and
enjoy

… …

I was promised heaven

I'm back into my body.
Alone.
Yet again in the dark.
The cup is still full.
What only a drop could do, terrible.
Still bubbling, still saccharine.
The elegant glassware against the wooden shelves.
A glass, which held the butterfly eggs.
Ready to implode inside the stomach.
Breaking the nests which housed them,
glide in the warm flesh walls.
Sugary water with hallucinations
spiced with wonder and pleasure.
Red, springs from my back.
Flows, imparting a route.
Glide down a hill, vertebrae.
Growl beyond the sight, pinning.
The needles are sharp, pressed.
His face looks amused, excited.
Grabbed with a collar and a whip
wiped my felicity away.
He is happy.
Hurts, hurts, hurts.
Am I broken?
He keeps replacing and relocating my bones,
hammered with hits and saw, screws and bolts.
Seam, seam, seam.
Like new!
The doll looked perfect again.
The gates were carnally closed with
ire and tether.
Cry, cry, cry.
Until I become dry.
He left again, now alone.
But it hurts too much.
The bruises in the face,
in th[e] hands, in the back.
I want them to disappear,
with me, with the void.
A trip to heaven, Utopia.
The glass' bubbles profess their love.
In dire need to attract a new audience.
The colors of the sky painted in the background,
the floor a forest and the walls a green.
Apparently, you brains can melt with love!
I'll take the uncaring appreciation of the fizzle and drizzle
a good sip happiness may allow!
For the better times, a toast to the nerves.

Yet this cheap alternative

flee, fly, flap like a butterfly
they blossom in my lungs and trachea!
a voyage inside hallways of quartz
the red root sprung
pop, pop, pop
they did in the plastic tube
the scent reminded me of bleach
blackening the dress, painted toucans
beaks of orange, declarations of juice
their relationship tore as a child by saw
as if pillars of flesh absorbed
amalga[m]ated with the red root
and white flowers
a bunch of rotting plants
squished into a glass of glossy wine
plump as the lips which caressed it
and in exchange the ecstasy of his nine tails
the spider puddled by the library
of the endless books and cacophony
of misleading onomatopoeia
you returned, you remembered
the appendix urging to carve
urging to path into the plastic bottle
desiring to conquer the red root
but its thorax was asymmetrical
therefore its legs were too
and its nine tails could only taste the skin joints
visualization of kaleidoscope's delusion
the crystals of ice sculpted
yet never, never again you wanted to conserve the dress
the toucans were like parasites, a nest
no butterfly could override
the plants curated into beverage
only a petition remains:

melt
into
the
sweet
sw[e]et
aroma
of
the
champagne-tasting
chlorinated
ever

wine

… …

Of chemistry and fake words

It's only a headache what persists.
A blurry picture laid over the floor.
Eyes reddish, burst into tears.
Again, yet again, the butterfly eggs.
I dared to eat them, now consequence.
He is asleep, I am suffering.
Inside a cell, reformed with cadmium.
No corrosion able to take me with it.
No air would ever change my fate.
I can't smile stuck with this psychopath.
Outside, the world shakes.
Only for me to be joined in leviathan.
He gifts, gifts me with his sickness.
Thinks flowers would heal a scar.
A beautiful dress would take away his juices.
Punishment is forgotten with love.
Stuck, stuck, he [w]ouldn't dare to show
after what he does, just
leave, leave, leave,
leave me alone rotting.
From the insides, a hematoma puddled.
Outside, the thin air adheres to my pores.
They exudate, combined with murk, orange.
My nexus, visceral, jumping to conclusions.
The broken veins deprive my heart of sense,
beginning to root.
It isn't plausible to imagine how
beautiful escape would be,
as his appendages would dig in my skin
hastily, his bare arms asymmetrical.
Imagine, imagine!
Chloroform sweetened with methanol down
my spine, later digested and eaten,
absorbed and captured.
Never let go of the sweetness.
It gives me chills,
to remember.
My lungs inhale, exhale,
greedy blood, poor taste.
Amber eyes cried.
I can't stop thinking, I can't!
Why won't I have a something to

… …

to…
to shut up.
to shut up!
to shut up, shut up!
blowing my fucking brains out
is the best solution.
such a second-hand meat doesn't suit me!
i deserve more than this.
whenever that guy is coming downstairs
i'll break him
decompose him
mutilate him
skin him!
so he feels an ounce of what i do
a pound of flesh p[a]id with a pound of flesh.
so, tell me, dear brains of mine…
whose head should i smash?
whose limbs are to be detached?
who deserves the collar and the whip?

… …

so i thought!
get ready, disgusting little shit.
you'll have the funniest day of them all.
the morning was assaulted by my sight
as soon as it dared to manifest.
deadly mourning of us, last time for him.
it was waking up time!
he'd get ready for his errands,
fastening the haste his host had.
the light was flickering.
vase in hand, grip tight.
reddened with somnolence,
deepened in purple and black.
adrenaline rushing through my flesh,
i could almost feel his insides.
the door creaked.

My nerves are confused

the vase hit him right in the face.
peeled off to reveal a little toad.
the toad was pronouncing its soliloquy,
every seam of his dress was broken.
the feather slowly glided towards a box.
closely!
such a scene felt like yearning.
reddened root, began to expand and contract,
as the glass slowly drifted down.
hurting, familiar smell,
already anticipating arrival of the [k]aleidoscope.
toucans flew high.
his dress laid abandoned, i tried to wear it.
it was stiff, pale, hardened.
the glass balls chanted in water.
a geyser opened them in half, a miracle
which only the spider's leg would house.
garden, in greenhouse capsule.
the rotten flowers asked the plum:
"my, my! so gorgeous in your eyes of violet!"
a dagger behind.
"oh, why my dear! the most precious of them all"
their thorns excited.
roots oozed a thick white-green cloud.
"it'll make you fly"
she wanted to fly, fly free!
therefore, the plum took a sip.
and another.
and another!
she couldn't stop laughing at the
pus and chlorophyll of the decaying roses.
"i love it when you giggle"
but she laughed because of her defective
fibers and confused dress.
"dance with us!"
their mov[e]s were too bright, too acidic.
what would the plum do?
invent her own dance.
tangled with a clear sky,
such grew tyrannical, arachnid legs.
thorax was coated behind the joints,
