This is what I want, dad.

Dad
Are you sure, sweetie?
No more gifts until next year—

Agatha
Just buy it and give it to me.

Just bought and given to you!

tO neural deficienCy?

Since then, the butterfly eggs carved
deep below my pores.
Every string of hair wa[s] made to
smoothen the laid-down eggs.
Cutaneous, I dare to think.
Arrived!
Just a drop, they say.
I'll try my new gift!
So the maelstrom was unle[a]shed from the different wrinkles.

To fake colors?

the wine began cascading!
stroboscopic glass
carbonated methanol
dancing with myself
amalgamated with arms and legs
the longing of the arachnid legs
butterflies now wingless
spring arrived to my alveolar cavities
a trip inside corridors of calcium deposits
it was when i came back
polydactyl caverns
never stop filling the bottle
shards in his mouth, eyes, ears
the frog hadn't developed legs
soliloquy of his
the house of green was a capsule
in state of decay, hearts of a garden
her eyes of violet were gorgeous
indiscernible from the above blue
with its arachnid legs, there, horizon
joints hid the thorax
a task never dared to be done
forever refused to vanish monthly red
she preferred to be consumed by maggots
and then black
and then white.

Was life made to be so bitter

It's unbearable to see my
skin necrose because of the drink.
Only thing that allows me to exit.
Only thing I care about.
He'd look me with this adipose face.
Over itself, a wavy beard intertwined.
Deeply black, hematomas were too.
Fistulas are just the new rot!
The drink sat there, the butterfly eggs.
They were about to be born serenely.
Innocently anchoring to my deep breath
and rushed towards exhalation.
But suddenly, green sprung.
Sprang acid all over the floor,
the undigested eggs back to genesis.
My sweet protective angel rushed.
The floor, what a mess!
He ignored the [v]aporizing substance and
sprinted towards me.
His ever-loving smile faded,
aside from the slightest hate.
He kept whispering to himself,
"I'm sorry, I'm sorry!"
the door slightly opened.
And the light was to hit me right in the face.

…

A real light, the sun.
The sun!
He looked pitiful at my trashed state,
at my disposable body.

……

Just this onc[e].

I am its victim

Merrily, my lachrymals into work.
It built up a tension,
my sinews so uptight they would almost tear.
The fresh air of the outside was almost there…!
The steps the only obstacle.

Step.
Step.
Step.

…?

My leg wouldn't budge.
It wouldn't move.
Be functional, please!
It negated horribly, sticking to ground.
Almost.
Almost!
It was STUPID to think that would work.
It was HORRIBLE to even consider escape a option.
Yet it wasn't he, it was ME.
If I had only waited a bit…
Father looked:

its scapegoat

Dad
Don't you want to escape?

……

Yet it was the collar of butterflies
and the whip of wine which wouldn't
let me do so.

Only one option

Dad
Agatha, listen to me!

Why had that [m]onster to appear?
Why wouldn't I live now in the village?
Why did I have to survive?

Dad
I guess you aren't in the mood…

Horridly abused

Yet I was and I wasn't.
yet I needed to yearn and needed
to cut my hope short.
Yet pollen in the air.

No return now

Dad
As you wish, my sweet dove.

……

The door closed again.

…

What now?
I just wonder,
why Dream Wine?
It was so dumb to hope!
It was so hurting to watch!
I can't. I can't. I can't!
The bottle alone.
The butterfly eggs!
A deep breath.
A superficial breath.
Readying my tongue.
Umami and drug down my throat!

Just forget
and melt
into the sweet
sweet
aroma of the
champagne-tasting
ever-shifting
concrete-like
chlorinated wine
and
enjoy!

Because there's no need to fight
for what pierced and skinned and mutilated.
How will this dream begin, end?
I'll keep your promise and
just make my corpse melt!
Everything down, the bottle dry.

I feel so beautiful?!

So i melted into The Dream!

the butterflies finally blossomed!
it was then my limbs detached,
now they sprung around the mausoleum
the croaking of the toad would
now never cease, his dress washed
and torn, now it was at dystopia
such a sacrifice he had done
to make me yearn so much for the hell
above!
my lymphatic member now in a
chorus, each of their infinitely small
voices dissected my shell
i was bare!
spiders, spiders, now crawled back
to their purgatory itself
to be consumed by the flesh
amalgamation, they would be bait just
for the idiotic thought of protecting me
yet i need no protection
from the poor maggots which
just search for home, for care!
from the thriving fungi,
only desiring to consume my red root.
the view distorted by ascending spheres,
i could surely recall
their popping, loudly enough to
enjoy a supper crawl of the plastic tube,
only the unleashed wrinkles were
to tease me with dystopia
glossy was the tempting,
chloroform, cheap-seamed visuals
coldly captured and combined and produced
for me to savor my tongue;
god, so fleshy, so thick!
blood down a new throat, what the
dream led me to
what the dream wanted, it wanted
to make my a coat of red,
a hood of white
so the blade of stars resonated deep into
my skin, a cutaneous substance which
mannered in pleasure
contour in pleasure as my skin oil
enters my venae cavae!
a hollow corridor only longing for more
sustenance never ending
cadmium and hollow plastic of a
stray laboratory deep in my brain,
in my voice
out!
out it tried to go as I expertly
maneuvered my arm and
gulp,
it suddenly started bleeding
and wouldn't stop,
maybe defective
dysfunctional!
so it started severing
maybe tearing would stop?
it didn't, it doesn't work
sadly, never would work
was a sharp sting that sucked my
exerted thoughts, exiled spirit
bulging obscenely through my eyes
in panic, the reservoir was to be depleted
a plum tried eating its own pulp,
trying to find a cure, nullifying her disease
the pulp of the plum of plump lips
decided it was better severing herself

mesh

the butterflies now flapped inside the cage,
and wanted to be free
the needed to be free
time to let them taste the sky i never saw
it was like needles, yet only a puncturing
object of moonlight and divinity, each
pore newly carved was a gateway to freedom
one by one,
outside my flesh,
they didn't belong to my precious dress,
the growing toucans parasitizing
a chill!
the coldness alerted the spider,
who rushed to save my decaying iron
bloody hands, knife
indiscernible inside such a mess
a mess I was led to by seducing vocables
and undeveloped mind
by horrid hope and his proper judgement
a deep, strong breath
futile attempt to measure the
ridiculous scenery
yet i could at least fulfill your promise!
i melted

into
the
sweet
sweet
aroma
of
the
champagne-tasting
chlorinated
ever
shifting
concrete-like
wine
and
enjoyed!

… …

so why do you look at me with horror,
dear dad, dear friend,
dear intruder in my song?
can't i house the parasitic
in myself?
it felt so good
it will always do
my only regret is to
not having brought him
with m[e]
into
the
sky!

ILikeChiskake

BRC-SFC.title: BRC-SFC
BRC-SFC.description:
BRC-SFC

The standard BRC-SFC with a 10 round magazine.

The BRC-SFC BRC-TZ (Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium-Small Arms Synchronization; Aurielleno: Buseleum Cowensorcio Resorase-Steco Fiuscil Cesueat) is an Auriellen pistol-caliber carbine chambered for the 9.0x18mm Aurum cartridge developed by the state-owned small arms research and development manufacturer Buseleum Reconnaisance Consortium based in Nereneavos.

The carbine was designed in synchronization with the Pistol Standardization Project initialized in 2011, and inital test trials of the carbine began in 2012 amongst the bases of Level 340 with testing completed near the end of 2013. Although originally intended for use with Auriellen security forces, the weapon has also seen usage with support troops in the Auriellen military, notably combat engineers and logistic drivers.

Object 305.title: Object 305
Object 305.description: The Rice Automatic is a blowback-operated submachine gun firing from an open bolt. Both the blowback-operation and open bolt systems are among the most rudimentary firing systems applicable to a repeater firearm. This results in a submachine gun that is extremely simple to mass produce and maintain, placing it within the capability for larger Backrooms societies to engineer and produce. The gun is made entirely of readily available and applicable metals, though steel is prioritized when available. Processes used to manufacture the gun include stamping, welding, and precise machining for the barrel, bolt, and firing mechanism. The ubiquitous usage of metal throughout the gun contributed to its heavy weight, being almost as heavy as the modern BRC-TZ, despite possessing a fraction of the firepower.
Object 305.lead:
Object 305
Rice Automatic

A Rice Automatic model SF79 used by Auriellen soldiers in Level 427, seen with a spare magazine and sling.

Type:Submachine gun

Organization of Origin:Liminarmory Inc.

In service:1974-present

Users:Major Explorer Group, Republic of Aurielle, Duchy of Cascada, Anti-Entity Group, Another Dimension Foundation, Architect Industries, Backroom Colonists, Backrooms Investigation Foundation, Backrooms Wanderers Organization, Kappa-IV Body, Republic of Level 153, Royal Exploration Unit, Party Crashers, and many other actors not listed in the Database.

Production history:
Designer: Oliver Rice, Clara Eubanks
Designed: 1971
Derived from:
Manufacturers: Liminarmory Inc, Republic of Aurielle, Major Explorer Group, Duchy of Cascada, and Anti-Entity Group
Produced: 1973-present
No. built: 160,000 to 220,000 across all variants

Specifications:
Mass: 3.57 kg (7.88 lb)
Length: 720 mm (28.3 in) with stock, 540 mm (21.2 in) with stock collapsed
Barrel length: 180 mm (7.08 in)
Cartridge: .45 ACP (11.43x23mm)
Action: Blowback, open bolt
Rate of fire: 450 rounds/min
Muzzle velocity: 260 meters/sec (853 feet/sec)
Feed system: 10-, 15, 20-, 25-, and 30-round box magazines, 40-, 50-, 60-, 75-, and 100-round drum magazines.

Overview

The Rice Automatic, commonly designated the RA100, is a submachine gun chambered in .45 ACP. It was developed by the Liminarmory as their flagship product in 1971 and went on to become one of the most widely used firearms across the Backrooms. Influential Backrooms groups such as the Major Explorer Group and Republic of Aurielle produce licensed copies of the Rice Automatic under special contracts.

Its cost-effective and straightforward design allows for somewhat steady mass production by more industrialized Backrooms organizations. Considering many other manmade ranged weapons in the Backrooms are crossbows, nailguns, air guns, and muskets, the Rice Automatic, firing smokeless powdered-bullets, are relatively advanced. The gun serves as the antithesis to the longer-ranged Kalthoff Rifle, as the Rice Automatic is best used in close quarters combat encountered in most Backrooms environments.

As Backrooms combat evolves, the Rice Automatic is slowly declining in usage over the 21st century as many major militaries adopt assault rifles and personal defense weapons. The Liminarmory themselves have minimized production of the SMG since 2010, as they move on to making Kalashnikov replicas. However, due to the cost of servicing such weapons, the uncomplicated submachine gun still stands as an effective, logistics-friendly option despite its underwhelming firepower.
Development and History

Up until the 1960s, weapons manufacturing in the Backrooms was relatively primitive as there were no successful attempts at creating smokeless powder cartridges nor any gun that fires them. The Liminarmory was the first known organization to recreate the advanced bullets by recycling spent casings and adding a primer, some propellant, and either a bullet or steel ball akin to ones used by air guns at the time. Only .45 ACP bullets were produced, since it and the 1911 are the most common Backrooms munitions. In the mid 1960s, the Liminarmory began development on a weapon that would complement their .45 ACP restorations, being the Rice Automatic.

Around the same time, the Republic of Aurielle were developing prototypes of bolt-action rifles which would evolve into the modern Kalthoff Rifle. The Liminarmory saw an opportunity as they knew well that the Auriellen developments would not suit well to closer ranges, which most Backrooms firefights occur in. Remembering the success of open-bolt submachine guns in the Frontrooms, especially in the , work immediately began on an SMG chambered in .45 ACP utilizing said open-bolt mechanisms for a simple and cheap design while still remaining effective. The Liminarmory chose to replicate the M3 Grease Gun, due to its slow fire rate, ergonomic simplicity, and .45 ACP chambering.

Predicting that the weapon would garner major interest from many clients, the Liminarmory announced that they were developing an open-bolt submachine gun. They manufactured rudimentary prototypes of the submachine gun to bolster public interest. Unsurprisingly, their predictions were met, and the company received major investments from Aurielle, Cascada, the Major Explorer Group, and various others. The investments significantly boosted the company's R&D capabilities, allowing them to further refine the weapon and develop their bullet recycling department to supplement the rising demand for the Rice Automatic.

When the Rice Automatic first entered production, demand for the weapon was so high that the Liminarmory's manufacturing capabilities were unable to meet them. To alleviate this issue, the Liminarmory approved licensed copies of the firearm to be developed by major organizations with similar industrial capabilities. Ironically, the initial high demand greatly raised the cost of the weapon, which was counterproductive to the gun's intended purpose. Fortunately, after a few years, as more individuals acquired the weapon, demand and cost mellowed to nominal levels.

As the Rice Automatic embarked on a meteoric rise, it was quickly seen among almost every military and human/humanoid fighting force throughout the Backrooms. By 1979, the Liminarmory alone were producing 5,000 examples per year on average for its entire history. Of the smokeless-powdered firearms used in the Backrooms, roughly 35-50% were some variant of the Rice Automatic. The revenue generated from the submachine gun allowed the Liminarmory to further refine their bullet restorations, as it had multiple issues regarding reliability and stopping power. They also expanded into refurbishing 5.56x45, 7.62x39, .22 Long Rifle, 7.62x51, and .357 Magnum.

Unsurprisingly, many smaller organizations and bandits have tried to make bastardized replicas of the submachine gun which were not as effective and cannot be mass produced. The Liminarmory decided not to take action against these groups as their heavily limited capabilities of recreating the weapon.

With the Rice Automatic becoming ubiquitous among fighting forces in the Backrooms, it eventually became a symbol for the state of humanity in the Backrooms, showcasing their perseverance and survival when in anomalous conditions, but also the turmoil that erupts when things get too comfortable.

However, the gun is not timeless, and it would be superseded by future developments that solved its glaring issues, mainly its slow rate of fire and weight. The Liminarmory developed experimental variants that boosted its rate of fire to seven hundred rounds per minute, but the power of the .45 ACP cartridge made for troublesome recoil for inexperienced shooters, and increased the strain on the weapon's delicate parts. In the late 1990s, various renowned gunsmiths attempted recreating assault rifles, particularly 7.62x39mm Kalashnikovs, which were successful but cannot see mass production. This was figured out by Aurielle and the M.E.G. by the turn of the millennium.

Despite the onset of assault rifle production among major Backrooms militaries, the Rice Automatic, due to its low cost and simple nature, would remain a staple of fighters until humanity's last conflict in the Backrooms. It is still produced by the Liminarmory until today, with minimal differences from the first renditions in the early 1970s. However, the Liminarmory has released a modernized version with slight weight reduction and modularity for various attachments.
Design Details

Yatozten Zedkviati.title: Yatozten Zedkviati
Yatozten Zedkviati.description:
YATOZTEN ZEDKVIATI
Ҵ=𐋄ⵅ-𐅋† ⴵᗑ=Ⴞⴽ-
Garden of Ncipetheraz ⸎ Year 416 / 1632

The bygone shouting of scurrying children free up her thoughts. The chit-chat of Old Birds in the next room ahead is faint. A fragmented reflection of 2 people talking in front of a calendar. Tick, tock. Ahead is a window using its sill to house a candle that's burning wrapped herbs judging by the whiffs of cooked rosemary. The drooping foliage caresses the wooden walls, and the rough shade of an overhang kisses the ground and façade. Rain clambers atop it. An inward foundation holds up the building. Tick, tock, tick—each rock is glued together with hardened dirt and held up by stabbing, angled pillars. The faint, dry blood staining the mortar is older than you are—tock. The gravel pavement is filled with faint marks of footsteps drilling into the ground, alongside the hugs of flower. It's barely wide enough to support a sleeping man with his arms shackled to the sides. Pillars rise to support the overhang ends. An underlying miasma of petrichor plays its piece. Tick, tock. Between the yard and the road is a massive, view-obscuring network of precisely sloped tubes that migrate the rainwater downstream. Sunlight shines through the slit, blistering the aqueduct in light and preaching as much of its word to the darkness as it can muster. The landscape is framed like a drawing. Tick, tock, tick, toc—

Erratic tolling. Occasional slams against hardwood. Identical noises cry. Her cold palms slip out of her chin as her arm comes down to rest. A flurry of pins and needles dance, rattling against her elbow. The air on everyone's face is coated in worry. Door-shutting dissipates through the halls. The ringing bell is held up by a poke of long string that's running through the side of every room, obscured by an embossing on the mundane bone-made cement wall. The open door sighs footsteps and stale air. Beside it is the afterimage of where the clock used to be, with the holes made to bolt it into the wall still visible and breathing. Below the artifacts is an image frame graced with legs, yet vandalized to an absurd degree by carved text that are abruptly cut by a pristine painting of the father of mathematics. Only her room is still open now. Footsteps are fast approaching. Stair leads up to an elevated stage with a margin for the centerpiece—multiple cabinets snuggled together, sitting below an angled surface that grabs onto the wall. On top is a clock, gashed on the face of a portrait that was welded into the wall and integrated deeply into the room's foundations. Little ripples of torn painting decorate the sides of it; and so do the ripples of a shoes’ hurrying steps, as the silence makes way for his lightly panted breathing, and finally ends with a clack of nails hitting the door. The near-nonexistent swing taps the table seated behind it. Sorry for being late ࠷ the professor said. His hand grabbed onto the edge of the door and slammed it back, crushing the resisting airflow in the room like a skull being popped. The locking mechanism thrashes the dent in the door frame as he lets off a light gasp. His face is against the wall. His breath comes back with a deep inhale. The exams are graded ࠷ he proclaimed loud enough to carve through the weirdly deafening and over-engineered locking mechanism before turning around and stepping high enough to ignore the stairs. He looks towards the students. Although, by all means, it's not terrible; what is God with no machine to love ࠷ his voice grew tired with every next syllable. I’ll get to it tomorrow, but ࠷ the class collectively calms down ࠷ while I'm sure that some of you won't be needing most of this this anyways, it's unfortunate that we have the daughter of a revolutionary that can't, for the life of her, comprehend basic factorial ࠷ he exclaims as he looks deadpan toward her. She retracts her arm, letting it fall and audibly smack her legs below the table. The class stares at her like soldiers to a bandit, yet she ignores their short-lived gaze and juts her head to a slight angle. I get that you have more important things to do, but this is not that hard—don't think that this doesn't include the rest of you either ࠷ he turns around to the cabinets, rattling them open and scrummaging through the items. However, given how consistently all of you have been suffering with simple concepts, I think it's fair to assume that it’s probably my fault and I have no reason to get angry at kids for my own failure. She brings her stance back. Is this sudden change of heart to do with the new education minister ࠷ a student quipped. Tick, tock, tick, tock, tick, tock. Yep ࠷ he said with the most blunt smirk imaginable that leaned into laughter. But maybe they're onto something and I'm curious to try regardless ࠷ he says, shivering upward and grunting with a whole bunch of carved blocks in his hand. He leaves them on the table and angles himself to face both the crowd and the blocks.

We'll be learning the basics of Yatozten today, and indeed, thanks to the education minister, there's something quite special I can show all of you later. Sorry ࠷ that's all he has to offer for his quickly-handed gift to her. He clears his throat before he commands ࠷ raise your hand if you know what 2+2 is. A flurry of thirty hands ascend at once. Ivish, what is it ࠷ he asks while pointing at her. Four. Correct ࠷ the words come out in a higher pitch voice as he nods his head lightly in tandem with widening eyes and a forming smirk. His lips smack and recalibrate ࠷ why ࠷ he asked. Because—tick, tock, tick, tock, tick—if you have one basket and both of your hands have two daggers, and you put them all inside the basket, you get ࠷ she slows down her words ࠷ four daggers inside the basket. He quickly refutes ࠷ you can't respond to a question about theory with empiricism. Her eyebrows raise. He shakes his head slightly ࠷ there are other levels, four daggers in a basket is true here, but it will never truly be four daggers in a basket. Oh ࠷ she agrees. He continues ࠷ sometimes, 2+2 is 5, or 6, or -58,936, and what this means is that what I've been teaching all of you is basically invalid if you go anywhere else, and yes ࠷ he gestures his hands while his voice ramps in speed ࠷ they implemented a new curriculum but for the sake of making this easier for everyone, we're stuck with the old one. But how does addition actually work if it's a variable and not a consistent operation ࠷ he questioned. He grabs a block with plus embroidered onto it, putting it on a higher slant of the table ࠷ raise your hands if you know what addition requires. They raise their hands once again, some more reluctantly than others. Vksoyora ࠷ he chose a student in the back, sitting on a table alone. Addition requires two numbers, including numbers manipulated by factorial and under square roots. Correct ࠷ the professor takes two more blocks—Ҵ and 𐋄—putting them on the left of addition, with considerable padding between the operator and the two letters. He places a one on the bottom right of the plus.

If, with this current understanding of addition, we were to try and solve 2+2, the result would be nothing, we would be unable to get any number out of it ࠷ he slams his hand on the table, leaning against it ࠷ at all. However ࠷ his hand drags along the surface, leaving it and grabbing a block with another plus ࠷ if we were to place this thing, called an instruction ࠷ his fingers clasp it, showing it off to the classroom ࠷ into what we have right now ࠷ the block hugs the two letters ࠷ we've created something interesting, because it doesn't matter if we try to solve 75+842,841 instead, or try to solve every single possible addition problem; it'll always, every single time, without fail, with no exception, be one. If we place four of these ࠷ one by one, he slots them around in a ring surrounding the operator symbol in the middle ࠷ the problem 2+2 will, by our understanding, be correct, yet 1+2 will equal four. So, how do we solve this ࠷ through his periphery, he sees someone raising their hand ࠷ that was a rhetorical question, I know you can't answer it correctly right now—anyways ࠷ his voice pacens with the anticipated drop of the student's hand ࠷ we could theoretically make multiple versions of addition that calculates every possible problem; however, the issue with this approach, is that we're not stupid. You see this number here ࠷ he questions while rapidly thrumming on the ‘one’ block hugging the operator symbol with his fingernail ࠷ something interesting happens when we replace this with a two—this now becomes eight. 2+2 is eight, 4+4 is eight, 1+1 is eight, everything is eight. Hseanephyo, since you were so inclined earlier, how about you answer this one instead. The student's mouth is left open as he leans toward the table, eyebrow arched up and eyes squeezed to the point where he could feel his lids. Tick, tock, tick, tock. The number represents the amount of times it loops ࠷ a classmate behind Hseanephyo whispers in his ear with the same volume of the cries of a man being burned alive. The number represen— ࠷ the professor stops him before he can continue. Just say it out loud next time ࠷ he continues ࠷ but yes, that's correct. That doesn't fully solve our issue of being unable to use one operator for everything, though. What we want to do instead is use a sub-operator ࠷ he swaps the two for a one, and takes another symbol; a random one from the pile—ⴽ—and places it down and retrieving two more duplicates of the prior Ҵ block. One occupies the left side, while another cuddles the ⴽ block. Three pluses were cleared, and the remaining one was moved to the newly-forming ring. He grabs, for each, two more ⴽs and Ҵs, and places them right next to each other in the addition ring.

＋1

[Ҵ,𐋄]
ⴽ[Ҵ]
ⴽ[𐋄]

ⴽҴ

[Ҵ]
+

You're free to raise your hand on this one—what will this do ࠷ he looks as the classroom all raise their hand in confidence. Ivish, you're up again. She momentarily rethinks her reasoning before trusting herself and stating her initial assumption ࠷ since the plus adds one, and the number beside the symbol in the middle represents how many times it loops, this ⴽ function loops Ҵ number of times, adding one every iteration, resulting in whatever number Ҵ is. ⴽ is called twice in the operator ࠷ she winds down ࠷ and this—tick, tock, tick, tock—results in 2+2 equaling four, because the two ⴽs are essentially combining into four plus ones ࠷ she squints. Indeed ࠷ the professor nods. He continues, pacing around the stage in circles with his arms crossed behind his back ࠷ now, if we plugged in any single addition problem, we would get the correct answer—2+2 will equal four, and 8+6 will equal fourteen. Ivish raises her hand before interjecting prematurely ࠷ if plus adds one, then 2+(-5) won't work because addition will interpret this as 2+5 and output seven rather than negative three, right ࠷ she asks. Sharp eye ࠷ the professor thrums quietly. She looks behind her, expecting a quip, only to see a face confused by her gaze. The professor clears his throat and a bulbous mass falls down it, bulging out from his skin. Pay attention, Ivish ࠷ he commands before returning to the subject ࠷ however, that is a pretty attentive observation and I was going to mention it next, but that's not actually the case because instructions are context-aware which means that plus can still understand that it needs to add up to a negative number. This will be more important in later classes ࠷ he sighs. Speaking of ࠷ he sits on the unobstructed part of the table ࠷ Yatozten is made up of only four instructions, those being ࠷ his hand raises, bringing a finger up for every item on the list ࠷ plus one, negative one, enter fractions by one, and exit fractions. He picks up 4 blocks, a plus, a minus, a right arrow, and a left arrow, showing them off in his palms ࠷ these are all of them, ordered respectively. Every single operator and sub-operator has 12 maximum instructions before having to loop.

He sets them down ࠷ before we get to the surprise, as we are running out of time, the reason why Yatozten exists is to be able to explain what an operator does in a written form. Without that, we can't keep a uniform language. Yatozten makes sure that 2+2 is always 2+2, but simultaneously makes sure that we know why 2+2 can equal five. Basically, it turns every operator into a variable, while also letting us rebuild them from scratch. Here's a teaser for what's to come ࠷ he clears out the board and completely rearranges it, introducing and reusing blocks.

!𐅜[Ҵ]

[Ҵ]
𐅜[Ҵ]

𐅜1

[Ҵ]
ⴽ[Ҵ]-

ⴽҴ

[Ҵ]
＋

This is factorial ࠷ he smiles and nods in response to the class’s confusion. Now ࠷ he drops down from the table and begins walking towards the door ࠷ get out of your seats, line-up behind me, don't make a ruckus, and follow me ࠷ he unlocks the door and begins to slowly walk through the school halls. A long strip of wooden floor on the sides of the school connects the classrooms together. Windows are few and far between. The view is that of rain and grass, rarely a building in construction or a house of any kind. A rotting shack standing atop a mountain. The walls are filled with the memory of signs and paintings, overwritten and redone with new ones to boot. Innocent children's drawings of Old Birds and landscapes and architecture and weapons. There's some remnants of signs yet to be taken down, echoing the vices of a dead machine. Dirt mortar fills the cracks. Multiple connected pieces of a long, wooden embossing snuggles where the wall and floor meet. Mostly empty bookshelves hug it, angled slightly forward, with their legs biting into or sitting against the emboss. Old copies of the Eternal Garden lean against the wall. A shanghaied urn of ash. Dust covers the scripture’s title, surrounding the clearing of a handprint. The afterimage of signs promising literature, now buried by the Salvation Manifesto. Grime. Stampeding through the dry air. The insides of his throat feel coated by grout, thoroughly mixed in a soup of wax and debris. They pass a classroom. Four more. One of them is vacant right now; the rest are under reconstruction. Their names, held on a steel rod puncturing the wall, wrapped in leaves to hold a hanging sign, are either hacksawed-out or vandalized—Love, the only remaining one reads. The end of the hallway has a door. Tables. Chairs. Cast away on its sides. It grew closer, and closer, and closer. The increasing rain engulfed the sound of footsteps. The door unlocks. A light creak, and a pull forward. A light, mineral oil smell dances on everyone's nose. This was the teacher's lounge room ࠷ he said.

The lounge room was decently big, about the size of a classroom if not a bit bigger. She remembers that right beside the entrance was a room with a singular, unbroken table, surrounded by chairs that the professor's would meet and discuss at. There was a canteen of sorts, too, where school staff could put food, drinks, and other such things. A big calendar and the same big table on the stage of the classroom. There was even a wheel of dried leaf manuscripts. Zedkwoyo tried destroying our knowledge on how to make this before his regime fell ࠷ he cheered. Crank. Like a man shackled in his sleep, banding shelves covered the walls, from head to toe, from arm to arm. Within were sitting boxes suffused with gears, covered in dials and sliders, holes and carvings. They're operators. The chests huddled closely. Nerves of cogs flow through the machine and wrap around the boxes. Their teeth barely skin them. Drums of cylinders with jutting appendages, spiralling cogs, square rods traversing openings, correction mechanisms. The shelves bend and engulf the right side of the room, kissing the wall of what used to be the teacher's meeting area. Four boxes labelled “memory” fill them. Two dials, both set to zero. The door to the conference room can't fully open. A singular lamp lights the new storage room. Addition to make sense of Level 1, multiplication fit for Level 79, variants of operators for levels that the students couldn't recognize, operators that they would never learn at all. This is what actual prowess looks like, not whatever the door locking mechanism is ࠷ he exclaimed ࠷ this can calculate every single possible twelve digit number, with twelve points of fraction accuracy, with any operator for any reason. It was created and invented by the father of mathematics to solve the issue of natural human error in calculating Yatozten ࠷ he walks up to the operation table ࠷ now, I'll be demonstrating two plus five factorial, if even one cog fails, the entire machine breaks. He walks up to the operation table, looking down at the dials and sliders and cranks, as his students approach and try to find room to stare behind his shoulder. He selects two from the base twelve slider, and spins the topmost crank. The gears thrum and rattle, slowly and patiently to make sure the students see it. Tick, tock, tick, tock. A dial on the side of the main machine—a row of ten boxes containing multiple twelve-toothed cogs—displays and saves two on the topmost tooth, leaving 0 on the cog beside it. He resets the input. Beside the slider labelled operators is one labelled modifier. He selects the factorial notation. The number slider is moved to five. He cranks it again. Tick, tock, tick, tock, tick, tock. The memory box on the right displays 5 on the main cog, alongside 11 on the left gear. He resets the input. He selects addition from the operator slider, and grabs the bottommost crank, slowly twisting and pulling. The gears produce a melody. The mineral oil whiff becomes a miasma. Dials everywhere change, shift, and rattle. You can see every single calculation that every single operator does, both in sound and numbers. Tick, tock, tick, tock, tick, tock, tick, tock. 122. Can someone here try to solve that for me ࠷ he asks. Tick, tock. The silence is deafening. Tick, tock, tick—factorial of five is 120, so 120+2 is 122 ࠷ Vksoyora stated. Correct.

The bells toll a final hymn for today.
Yatozten Zedkviati.lead:
YATOZTEN ZEDKVIATI
Ҵ=𐋄ⵅ-𐅋† ⴵᗑ=Ⴞⴽ-
Garden of Ncipetheraz ⸎ Year 416 / 1632

The bygone shouting of scurrying children free up her thoughts. The chit-chat of Old Birds in the next room ahead is faint. A fragmented reflection of 2 people talking in front of a calendar. Tick, tock. Ahead is a window using its sill to house a candle that's burning wrapped herbs judging by the whiffs of cooked rosemary. The drooping foliage caresses the wooden walls, and the rough shade of an overhang kisses the ground and façade. Rain clambers atop it. An inward foundation holds up the building. Tick, tock, tick—each rock is glued together with hardened dirt and held up by stabbing, angled pillars. The faint, dry blood staining the mortar is older than you are—tock. The gravel pavement is filled with faint marks of footsteps drilling into the ground, alongside the hugs of flower. It's barely wide enough to support a sleeping man with his arms shackled to the sides. Pillars rise to support the overhang ends. An underlying miasma of petrichor plays its piece. Tick, tock. Between the yard and the road is a massive, view-obscuring network of precisely sloped tubes that migrate the rainwater downstream. Sunlight shines through the slit, blistering the aqueduct in light and preaching as much of its word to the darkness as it can muster. The landscape is framed like a drawing. Tick, tock, tick, toc—

Erratic tolling. Occasional slams against hardwood. Identical noises cry. Her cold palms slip out of her chin as her arm comes down to rest. A flurry of pins and needles dance, rattling against her elbow. The air on everyone's face is coated in worry. Door-shutting dissipates through the halls. The ringing bell is held up by a poke of long string that's running through the side of every room, obscured by an embossing on the mundane bone-made cement wall. The open door sighs footsteps and stale air. Beside it is the afterimage of where the clock used to be, with the holes made to bolt it into the wall still visible and breathing. Below the artifacts is an image frame graced with legs, yet vandalized to an absurd degree by carved text that are abruptly cut by a pristine painting of the father of mathematics. Only her room is still open now. Footsteps are fast approaching. Stair leads up to an elevated stage with a margin for the centerpiece—multiple cabinets snuggled together, sitting below an angled surface that grabs onto the wall. On top is a clock, gashed on the face of a portrait that was welded into the wall and integrated deeply into the room's foundations. Little ripples of torn painting decorate the sides of it; and so do the ripples of a shoes’ hurrying steps, as the silence makes way for his lightly panted breathing, and finally ends with a clack of nails hitting the door. The near-nonexistent swing taps the table seated behind it. Sorry for being late ࠷ the professor said. His hand grabbed onto the edge of the door and slammed it back, crushing the resisting airflow in the room like a skull being popped. The locking mechanism thrashes the dent in the door frame as he lets off a light gasp. His face is against the wall. His breath comes back with a deep inhale. The exams are graded ࠷ he proclaimed loud enough to carve through the weirdly deafening and over-engineered locking mechanism before turning around and stepping high enough to ignore the stairs. He looks towards the students. Although, by all means, it's not terrible; what is God with no machine to love ࠷ his voice grew tired with every next syllable. I’ll get to it tomorrow, but ࠷ the class collectively calms down ࠷ while I'm sure that some of you won't be needing most of this this anyways, it's unfortunate that we have the daughter of a revolutionary that can't, for the life of her, comprehend basic factorial ࠷ he exclaims as he looks deadpan toward her. She retracts her arm, letting it fall and audibly smack her legs below the table. The class stares at her like soldiers to a bandit, yet she ignores their short-lived gaze and juts her head to a slight angle. I get that you have more important things to do, but this is not that hard—don't think that this doesn't include the rest of you either ࠷ he turns around to the cabinets, rattling them open and scrummaging through the items. However, given how consistently all of you have been suffering with simple concepts, I think it's fair to assume that it’s probably my fault and I have no reason to get angry at kids for my own failure. She brings her stance back. Is this sudden change of heart to do with the new education minister ࠷ a student quipped. Tick, tock, tick, tock, tick, tock. Yep ࠷ he said with the most blunt smirk imaginable that leaned into laughter. But maybe they're onto something and I'm curious to try regardless ࠷ he says, shivering upward and grunting with a whole bunch of carved blocks in his hand. He leaves them on the table and angles himself to face both the crowd and the blocks.

We'll be learning the basics of Yatozten today, and indeed, thanks to the education minister, there's something quite special I can show all of you later. Sorry ࠷ that's all he has to offer for his quickly-handed gift to her. He clears his throat before he commands ࠷ raise your hand if you know what 2+2 is. A flurry of thirty hands ascend at once. Ivish, what is it ࠷ he asks while pointing at her. Four. Correct ࠷ the words come out in a higher pitch voice as he nods his head lightly in tandem with widening eyes and a forming smirk. His lips smack and recalibrate ࠷ why ࠷ he asked. Because—tick, tock, tick, tock, tick—if you have one basket and both of your hands have two daggers, and you put them all inside the basket, you get ࠷ she slows down her words ࠷ four daggers inside the basket. He quickly refutes ࠷ you can't respond to a question about theory with empiricism. Her eyebrows raise. He shakes his head slightly ࠷ there are other levels, four daggers in a basket is true here, but it will never truly be four daggers in a basket. Oh ࠷ she agrees. He continues ࠷ sometimes, 2+2 is 5, or 6, or -58,936, and what this means is that what I've been teaching all of you is basically invalid if you go anywhere else, and yes ࠷ he gestures his hands while his voice ramps in speed ࠷ they implemented a new curriculum but for the sake of making this easier for everyone, we're stuck with the old one. But how does addition actually work if it's a variable and not a consistent operation ࠷ he questioned. He grabs a block with plus embroidered onto it, putting it on a higher slant of the table ࠷ raise your hands if you know what addition requires. They raise their hands once again, some more reluctantly than others. Vksoyora ࠷ he chose a student in the back, sitting on a table alone. Addition requires two numbers, including numbers manipulated by factorial and under square roots. Correct ࠷ the professor takes two more blocks—Ҵ and 𐋄—putting them on the left of addition, with considerable padding between the operator and the two letters. He places a one on the bottom right of the plus.

If, with this current understanding of addition, we were to try and solve 2+2, the result would be nothing, we would be unable to get any number out of it ࠷ he slams his hand on the table, leaning against it ࠷ at all. However ࠷ his hand drags along the surface, leaving it and grabbing a block with another plus ࠷ if we were to place this thing, called an instruction ࠷ his fingers clasp it, showing it off to the classroom ࠷ into what we have right now ࠷ the block hugs the two letters ࠷ we've created something interesting, because it doesn't matter if we try to solve 75+842,841 instead, or try to solve every single possible addition problem; it'll always, every single time, without fail, with no exception, be one. If we place four of these ࠷ one by one, he slots them around in a ring surrounding the operator symbol in the middle ࠷ the problem 2+2 will, by our understanding, be correct, yet 1+2 will equal four. So, how do we solve this ࠷ through his periphery, he sees someone raising their hand ࠷ that was a rhetorical question, I know you can't answer it correctly right now—anyways ࠷ his voice pacens with the anticipated drop of the student's hand ࠷ we could theoretically make multiple versions of addition that calculates every possible problem; however, the issue with this approach, is that we're not stupid. You see this number here ࠷ he questions while rapidly thrumming on the ‘one’ block hugging the operator symbol with his fingernail ࠷ something interesting happens when we replace this with a two—this now becomes eight. 2+2 is eight, 4+4 is eight, 1+1 is eight, everything is eight. Hseanephyo, since you were so inclined earlier, how about you answer this one instead. The student's mouth is left open as he leans toward the table, eyebrow arched up and eyes squeezed to the point where he could feel his lids. Tick, tock, tick, tock. The number represents the amount of times it loops ࠷ a classmate behind Hseanephyo whispers in his ear with the same volume of the cries of a man being burned alive. The number represen— ࠷ the professor stops him before he can continue. Just say it out loud next time ࠷ he continues ࠷ but yes, that's correct. That doesn't fully solve our issue of being unable to use one operator for everything, though. What we want to do instead is use a sub-operator ࠷ he swaps the two for a one, and takes another symbol; a random one from the pile—ⴽ—and places it down and retrieving two more duplicates of the prior Ҵ block. One occupies the left side, while another cuddles the ⴽ block. Three pluses were cleared, and the remaining one was moved to the newly-forming ring. He grabs, for each, two more ⴽs and Ҵs, and places them right next to each other in the addition ring.

+

You're free to raise your hand on this one—what will this do ࠷ he looks as the classroom all raise their hand in confidence. Ivish, you're up again. She momentarily rethinks her reasoning before trusting herself and stating her initial assumption ࠷ since the plus adds one, and the number beside the symbol in the middle represents how many times it loops, this ⴽ function loops Ҵ number of times, adding one every iteration, resulting in whatever number Ҵ is. ⴽ is called twice in the operator ࠷ she winds down ࠷ and this—tick, tock, tick, tock—results in 2+2 equaling four, because the two ⴽs are essentially combining into four plus ones ࠷ she squints. Indeed ࠷ the professor nods. He continues, pacing around the stage in circles with his arms crossed behind his back ࠷ now, if we plugged in any single addition problem, we would get the correct answer—2+2 will equal four, and 8+6 will equal fourteen. Ivish raises her hand before interjecting prematurely ࠷ if plus adds one, then 2+(-5) won't work because addition will interpret this as 2+5 and output seven rather than negative three, right ࠷ she asks. Sharp eye ࠷ the professor thrums quietly. She looks behind her, expecting a quip, only to see a face confused by her gaze. The professor clears his throat and a bulbous mass falls down it, bulging out from his skin. Pay attention, Ivish ࠷ he commands before returning to the subject ࠷ however, that is a pretty attentive observation and I was going to mention it next, but that's not actually the case because instructions are context-aware which means that plus can still understand that it needs to add up to a negative number. This will be more important in later classes ࠷ he sighs. Speaking of ࠷ he sits on the unobstructed part of the table ࠷ Yatozten is made up of only four instructions, those being ࠷ his hand raises, bringing a finger up for every item on the list ࠷ plus one, negative one, enter fractions by one, and exit fractions. Object 6.title: Object 6
Object 6.description:
Moth Jelly is a nutritional, jelly-like substance produced by Death Moths to feed their young and maintain their hives. It typically takes on a violet coloration and has a similar consistency to honey although thinner. Moth Jelly produced by female Deathmoths has trace amounts of strong pheromones that attract male Deathmoths and cause them to enter an entranced state when consumed. This makes the male Deathmoth addicted to it and making them completely subservient to a particular hive's Queen and willing to do whatever the Queen wishes in exchange for more.

The main source of Moth Jelly is of course, female Deathmoths. The highly aggressive nature of female Death Moths makes trying to obtain Moth Jelly a dangerous and life-threatening endeavor, but the nutritious properties of the jelly makes it a reward that possibly outweighs the risk.

Moth Jelly can be found in any level with a high population of Deathmoths. Although rare, Moth Jelly packaged in bottles and jars also appear all throughout the Backrooms even where Deathmoths are not present. These are often labeled with a sticker reading "Property of Royal Jelly Farms™" accompanied by a logo of a purple crown.
Object 6.lead: Moth Jelly, also known as Queen Moth Jelly or Royal Jelly is a substance found in the Backrooms.

Object 10.title: Object 10
Object 10.description:
in life is the inevitability of death

and from death rises life once more

through caps, stalks, and spores

  ➢ DESCRIPTION

Backshrooms (known otherwise by the taxonomic name Aregnaceae) comprise a diverse family of mushrooms residing exclusively in the Backrooms. Although Backshrooms are living organisms, they are categorized as an object due to first being documented as such; the title merely remaining for simplicity's sake. The family is separated into 4 genera with a total of 12 known species.

Backshrooms are distinguished from typical mushrooms by the pattern under their caps. As the spore-carrying gills extend to the tip of the cap, they join into pairs at the ends to resemble chevron arrows — alike to those found on Level 0's wallpaper. Backshrooms are present in nearly all levels of the Backrooms in varying population levels, though environments with more readily available nutrients (i.e. containing an abundance of soil, decayed organic matter, etc.) are more likely to cultivate a greater diversity of Backshrooms. Most Backshrooms are highly symbiotic with other species or otherwise reactive with objects unique to the Backrooms.

ANOMALOUS MICROORGANISMS (XENOPLANKTON)
The family Aregnaceae stemmed from a single mushroom ancestor that noclipped from Earth to the Backrooms. Because nutrients in the Backrooms were far less readily available, requiring extremely specialized environments or traits, the family of mushrooms was pressured to undergo severe evolutionary changes to survive. Consequently, they developed more sophisticated means of nutrient storage and opened themselves to a broader diet. As their microbiomes reached a certain transitionary stage, the mushrooms had coincidentally created a viable environment for entities known as Xenoplankton.

Xenoplankton, alongside all other anomalous life of the Backrooms, fall under the group Anomalobasia; these lifeforms are characterized by psychodominous bonds which allow their properties to bypass the typical laws of baseline reality. While some anomalous entities grew into larger niches, Xenoplankton shrunk to microscopic sizes at approximately 50 micrometers in diameter. These anomalous microorganisms, though simple, can still effectively thrive in nearly all environments of the Backrooms through their rapid reproductive rate and wide range of properties. Because mutations are exceedingly prevalent in Xenoplankton, they can bypass reality in a number of ways to their advantage.

  ➢ DOMESTIC USAGE
Use cases for Backshrooms are regularly posted by wanderers on Shroomsite, embedded on this article for quick viewing: backnet154://shroomsite.net

  ➢ SPECIES

GENUS: PSYCHOMITUS

The genus Psychomitus is characterized by caps that possess chemicals specialized towards affecting the minds of animals. Members of this genus typically have thick pale stalks and grow on dead organic matter.

PSYCHOMITUS RELICIENS // CINNAMON SHROOMSCLASS: EDIBLE HAZARD: 0/3
----

Cinnamon Shrooms are one of the most common species of Backshrooms, growing in most of the Main Nine levels. They are identified by their smooth, pale-brown caps which smell vaguely of cinnamon, along with off-white stalks that typically root from steep surfaces such as trees or earthy walls. As per their namesake, Cinnamon Shroom caps have a sweet, cinnamon-like taste, alongside providing stress-relieving effects when consumed. The microbiomes of Cinnamon Shrooms primarily assist in rapidly reproducing and spreading across various environments.

PSYCHOMITUS INSANIAE // INSANITY SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 3/3
----

Insanity Shrooms are most commonly found in Level 626, possessing a dimpled magenta exterior. Typically in contrast to their environment due to their vibrant coloring, Insanity Shrooms are small yet potent carriers of hallucinogenic and mentally disorienting substances. These effects include hallucinations, a helpless sense of fear, and loss of energy. The chemicals are emitted passively through sacs filled with a purple gas along the stalk's exterior, which release upon being disturbed by organisms that would feed from them. However, the sacs often pop without disturbance, causing adjacent air to large clusters of Insanity Shrooms to be intoxicated by their psychoactive substances.

PSYCHOMITUS AUREUS // MARIGOLD SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 0/3
----

Marigold Shrooms are one of the more reactive species in the genus Psychomitus, with many resulting products being used for medicinal purposes. The cap is a bright and reflective yellow hue and becomes concave towards the center; at the tip, special chemicals are produced which repulse some species of fungivorous entities. While rare (only found in certain levels and specific conditions), Marigold Shrooms are useful in the production of many medicinal objects. Reactions can occur by placing the desired object in the "cup" of the live mushroom and lightly pricking the stalk with a sharp object.

Placing Moth Jelly in the cup of a Marigold Shroom and waiting approximately eight hours will result in a thin, dark-yellow translucent fluid. Drinking this mixture can improve symptoms of various diseases and mitigate contraction upon exposure to small enough amounts of Sanguine Festivus or Hydrolitis Plague.

Placing Mixologist's Delight in the cup of a Marigold Shroom and waiting approximately two hours will result in a more vibrant shade and the emission of colorless fumes. Inhalation of the resulting fumes can assist in the repair of skin and muscle tissue, often used in treating wanderers following entity attacks.

GENUS: EUSYMBIOTIAE

Mushrooms of the genus Eusymbiotiae have more specialized microbiomes to support Xenoplankton than other species of Aregnaceae. Backshrooms in this genus also possess folds at the base of their stalks that hold clumps of mycelium.

EUSYMBIOTIAE DISTORTIA // GLITCH SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 2/3
----

Glitch Shrooms are a species of Backshrooms far larger than other members of the Aregnaceae family, at a maximum of 70 cm (2.2ft) in height. Caps and stalks of Glitch Shrooms can possess any color independent of each other, with rough speckles along the brim of the cap that are always roughly white. The cap is adorned with a singular marking that resembles a human eye, shifting itself around the surface to face any sentient organisms viewing it. In the case that multiple sentient beings are looking at one Glitch Shroom at once, it will appear to face only the viewer in each of their perspectives; how and why Glitch Shrooms do this is entirely unknown.

Glitch Shrooms provide an unusually healthy environment for their microbiomes, directing approximately a quarter of their received nutrients to sustaining internal life. The Xenoplankton within are able to heavily destabilize reality within a limited proximity of the mushroom itself.

EUSYMBIOTIAE FERROS // IRON SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 1/3
----

Iron Shrooms are small orange-hued mushrooms that grow in dark, metallic levels (Level 2, Level 8.1, etc.). Their caps are speckled with small spikes that are turned rust-colored by their high iron content. Iron Shrooms tend to cluster around areas with dense saturations of metal, using specialized microbial feeding networks to "amplify" the nutrition received from feeding on metal and grime. A mostly pure chunk of iron develops in the stalks of Iron Shrooms as they age, being one of the primary sources of metal in the Backrooms. Many groups harvest and even cultivate this species for their consistent supply of iron.

EUSYMBIOTIAE CARNIA // FLESH SHROOMSCLASS: EDIBLE HAZARD: 0/3
----

Flesh Shrooms grow in stacked "columns" along living or non-living surfaces, characterized by their fleshy color and thin leaflike shape. As per their namesake, Flesh Shrooms are genetically encoded with an unidentifiable mammal's muscle DNA, resulting in Flesh Shrooms having many properties akin to animal meat. Flesh Shrooms, when properly cooked, have a taste described as a somewhat sweet blend of steak, bacon, and goat meat. Flesh Shrooms are commonly eaten by many wanderers across the Backrooms for their accessibility and taste in comparison to other available foods.

EUSYMBIOTIAE ARGENTUM // SILVER SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 3/3
----

Silver Shrooms tend to grow from living hosts such as trees or mosses and are identified by their prominent silver shade. Silver Shrooms are known to be obligately symbiotic with Silver Slimes; when a Silver Slime comes within range of a non-hosting Silver Shroom, it is drawn in by the warmth and chemicals emitted by the mushroom's gills. Silver Shrooms redirect the slime to its system of hyphae (or "roots" of mushrooms) and absorb it into specialized chambers. When the Silver Slime detects a small organism on the surface and begins moving towards it, the host is alerted and feels for the organism in question to eject the slime onto. The prey provides both organisms with food, and both parties are benefitted as a result. Consumption or contact with Silver Shrooms can lead to traces of Silver Slime entering the body and consuming the hemoglobin inside, typically resulting in death.

EUSYMBIOTIAE HALLUCINARI // AETHER SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 3/3
----

Aether Shrooms are matte-purple Backshrooms with a velvety exterior, often enveloped by a faint bright aura. Aether Shrooms use specialized Xenoplankton to warp nearby space upon being deformed, causing any object that breaks the mushroom to warp into itself. Although this trait exists to mutilate threats attempting to eat the mushroom, Aether Shrooms' "space-defining" properties can be exploited to a wanderer's own advantage.

Spatial warping boundaries are projected in outward rays across the mushroom's surface and only activate if the Aether Shroom's body from the stalk upwards is deformed. Uprooting Aether Shrooms at the base of the mushroom body will not cause it to warp space, and therefore will allow one to carry Aether Shrooms without harm. Throwing an uprooted Aether Shroom at a threat (such as a hostile entity) will cause the mushroom to squash on contact, permanently mutilating the entity and allowing escape.

GENUS: PROTERTIA

The genus Protertia, like Eusymbiotiae, is defined by deep folds at the base of their stalks, however without clumps of mycelium held within. These folds instead hold a stringy substance that assists the mushroom in finding suitable places for the spores to root.

PROTERTIA ADJACIS // WINDOW SHROOMSCLASS: EDIBLE HAZARD: 2/3
----

Window Shrooms are the only living species of the genus Protertia, being thin-stalked translucent mushrooms that grow along the frames of Predatory Windows. Within the stalk folds of Window Shrooms are thin stringy hairs coated in space-sensing Xenoplankton that guide the spores to a suitable growing location, which are ejected when it wishes to reproduce. Window Shrooms primarily grow on the frames of Window entities, which wait for the Window to draw in prey before beginning growth. As the prey is consumed, whether it be human or other, it leaves residual traces of psychic energy encoded with the prey's thoughts. Window Shrooms consume the energy and adapt specialized behaviors to draw in more prey of that kind (often by a produced scent or other chemical trace), which the Window recognizes and rewards with parts of future prey's corpses. Though hazardous to collect, Window Shrooms are edible and contain many essential nutrients.

GENUS: AMNIROFILA

Backshrooms in the genus Amnirofila have a specialized network of nutrient-delivering fibers in the stalk's outer ring, with these fibers visibly growing out along the cap's concave side.

AMNIROFILA KHARTHIA // KHARTHIC SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 2/3
----

Kharthic Shrooms have a dark, iridescent exterior that encapsulates the entirety of its surface body. They are relatively small and have stubby stalks, growing rarely in the open corners of most interior levels. Modified spore structures within Kharthic Shrooms carry a concentrated quantity of high-frequency Kharthic Energy which are released when threatened — causing the typical effects of Kharthiact exposure to the mind when inhaled. Consuming Kharthic Shroom spores can swiftly lead to severe activations in the "fight or flight" response and mental disorientation. It is not advised to eat Kharthic Shrooms raw in hopes of keeping one alert; instead, this effect can be obtained less hazardously by consuming Moth Jelly mixed with ground-up Kharthic Shroom stalks.

AMNIROFILA IGNIS // PYRO SHROOMSCLASS: EDIBLE HAZARD: 1/3
----

Pyro Shrooms are tall, warm-hued Backshrooms appearing commonly throughout dark areas. Often appearing in a bright exterior red, Pyro Shrooms tend to stand out from their environment as a form of threat display. The stalk turns yellow at the base through a gradient, wherein filter-like pores cover the base structure. When agitated at these pores (naturally through chemical reception of fungivorous entities), the Pyro Shroom in question diverts nearby heat and oxygen to a pinpoint location on the perceived threat. This process often causes the creation of a controlled flame or extreme heat on the threat's body. With the proper technique, wanderers can use Pyro Shrooms' defensive measures as an efficient firestarter.

AMNIROFILA REPLENS // CLONE SHROOMSCLASS: DEPENDENT EDIBILITY HAZARD: 0/3
----

Clone Shrooms are stout bulbous mushrooms that grow in two distinct stages throughout a life cycle. Clone Shrooms generally retain little of their own biomass; up to 20% of their body weight may be comprised of Xenoplankton at a given time. Upon the first stage, the Clone Shroom may be rooted in any substrate or material, as long as it is solid and hyphae are able to extend through it. In the first stage, Clone Shrooms bear microscopic hairs along their caps which are used to catch stray floating Xenoplankton and gain nutrients.

The second stage involves internal replication of the Clone Shroom's substrate, wherein the surface it grows on will be reproduced in the hollow chambers of the spherical structure. Many theories have circulated regarding why this occurs in Clone Shrooms, though it is believed to be a vestigial trait once necessary in reproduction; on standard substrate, such as soil, this would thereby replicate the nutrients in the soil, which are necessary for the Clone Shroom's stalk to grow rapidly and spread spores further. To an extent, any material meeting the preceding criteria can be cloned through a Clone Shroom's life cycle.
Object 10.lead:
in life is the inevitability of death

and from death rises life once more

through caps, stalks, and spores

  ➢ DESCRIPTION

Backshrooms (known otherwise by the taxonomic name Aregnaceae) comprise a diverse family of mushrooms residing exclusively in the Backrooms. Although Backshrooms are living organisms, they are categorized as an object due to first being documented as such; the title merely remaining for simplicity's sake. The family is separated into 4 genera with a total of 12 known species.

Backshrooms are distinguished from typical mushrooms by the pattern under their caps. As the spore-carrying gills extend to the tip of the cap, they join into pairs at the ends to resemble chevron arrows — alike to those found on Level 0's wallpaper. Backshrooms are present in nearly all levels of the Backrooms in varying population levels, though environments with more readily available nutrients (i.e. containing an abundance of soil, decayed organic matter, etc.) are more likely to cultivate a greater diversity of Backshrooms. Most Backshrooms are highly symbiotic with other species or otherwise reactive with objects unique to the Backrooms.

ANOMALOUS MICROORGANISMS (XENOPLANKTON)
The family Aregnaceae stemmed from a single mushroom ancestor that noclipped from Earth to the Backrooms. Because nutrients in the Backrooms were far less readily available, requiring extremely specialized environments or traits, the family of mushrooms was pressured to undergo severe evolutionary changes to survive. Consequently, they developed more sophisticated means of nutrient storage and opened themselves to a broader diet. As their microbiomes reached a certain transitionary stage, the mushrooms had coincidentally created a viable environment for entities known as Xenoplankton.

Xenoplankton, alongside all other anomalous life of the Backrooms, fall under the group Anomalobasia; these lifeforms are characterized by psychodominous bonds which allow their properties to bypass the typical laws of baseline reality. While some anomalous entities grew into larger niches, Xenoplankton shrunk to microscopic sizes at approximately 50 micrometers in diameter. These anomalous microorganisms, though simple, can still effectively thrive in nearly all environments of the Backrooms through their rapid reproductive rate and wide range of properties. Because mutations are exceedingly prevalent in Xenoplankton, they can bypass reality in a number of ways to their advantage.

  ➢ DOMESTIC USAGE
Use cases for Backshrooms are regularly posted by wanderers on Shroomsite, embedded on this article for quick viewing: backnet154://shroomsite.net

  ➢ SPECIES

GENUS: PSYCHOMITUS

The genus Psychomitus is characterized by caps that possess chemicals specialized towards affecting the minds of animals. Members of this genus typically have thick pale stalks and grow on dead organic matter.

----

Cinnamon Shrooms are one of the most common species of Backshrooms, growing in most of the Main Nine levels. They are identified by their smooth, pale-brown caps which smell vaguely of cinnamon, along with off-white stalks that typically root from steep surfaces such as trees or earthy walls. As per their namesake, Cinnamon Shroom caps have a sweet, cinnamon-like taste, alongside providing stress-relieving effects when consumed. The microbiomes of Cinnamon Shrooms primarily assist in rapidly reproducing and spreading across various environments.

PSYCHOMITUS INSANIAE // INSANITY SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 3/3
----

Insanity Shrooms are most commonly found in Level 626, possessing a dimpled magenta exterior. Typically in contrast to their environment due to their vibrant coloring, Insanity Shrooms are small yet potent carriers of hallucinogenic and mentally disorienting substances. These effects include hallucinations, a helpless sense of fear, and loss of energy. The chemicals are emitted passively through sacs filled with a purple gas along the stalk's exterior, which release upon being disturbed by organisms that would feed from them. However, the sacs often pop without disturbance, causing adjacent air to large clusters of Insanity Shrooms to be intoxicated by their psychoactive substances.

PSYCHOMITUS AUREUS // MARIGOLD SHROOMSCLASS: NON-EDIBLE HAZARD: 0/3
----

Marigold Shrooms are one of the more reactive species in the genus Psychomitus, with many resulting products being used for medicinal purposes. The cap is a bright and reflective yellow hue and becomes concave towards the center; at the tip, special chemicals are produced which repulse some species of fungivorous entities. While rare (only found in certain levels and specific conditions), Marigold Shrooms are useful in the production of many medicinal objects. Reactions can occur by placing the desired object in the "cup" of the live mushroom and lightly pricking the stalk with a sharp object.

Placing Moth Jelly in the cup of a Marigold Shroom and waiting approximately eight hours will result in a thin, dark-yellow translucent fluid. Drinking this mixture can improve symptoms of various diseases and mitigate contraction upon exposure to small enough amounts of Sanguine Festivus or Hydrolitis Plague.

Placing Mixologist's Delight in the cup of a Marigold Shroom and waiting approximately two hours will result in a more vibrant shade and the emission of colorless fumes. Inhalation of the resulting fumes can assist in the repair of skin and muscle tissue, often used in treating wanderers following entity attacks.

Object 2.title: Object 2
Object 2.description:
For every numbered and anomalous level in the Backrooms, there is a level key, which varies in appearance depending on its respective level. Said level is identified by a runic symbol engraved into a small rectangular label on the handle of the key, or other similar area in event of a uniquely-shaped key. The symbol on each key for each level is unique, corresponding with the symbols above their respective level doors within The Hub.

Due to each level key bearing a unique design, not all of them are represented as standard keys like those found in The Frontrooms. In certain instances, some may manifest in more abstract forms, unpredictable in function. They may even present themselves as other objects or entities.
Object 2.lead: Object 2, commonly referred to as level keys, are extremely rare objects found across the Backrooms which access the entrances of levels.

The Talking Box.title: The Talking Box
The Talking Box.description:
Talking Box is an entity that looks like a small rectangular black box made up of steel, small enough to fit in some guy's pocket. It also has a blue circle in the middle of its frame. The Talking Box has the ability to talk, obviously, and has other strange properties by glowing blue light which is create many things such as weapons or other stuff as long that isn't something organic either foods or even something alive is impossible to this entity.

An artificial intelligence-based object or entity. It is a small black rectangular metallic object with a faint blue glow at its center, which houses the artificial intelligence. The ability of this object to speak and interact with any digital and electronic devices via the signal it emits is its most noticeable feature.

An object or entity of sophisticated artificial intelligence of extradimensional origins. It is a small black rectangular metallic object in the center of its frame, with a faint blue glowing circle above it. It is where its other functions for its use, and for it to use, and is housed as a vital processing part of its systems, and possibly where AI resides within. The most noticeable feature of this object is its ability to communicate and interact with any digital and electronic devices via the signal it emits.

The object contains an abundance of pertinent data, particularly about the entire Backrooms, implying that this object and its unknown owners have or had more knowledge than any information in the Backrooms.

This object often refers to itself as I.A.M.s or Intelligence Apparatus Modular systems (pronounced as in "I am"), and the object stated that each of them took their specific roles and functions to their corresponding tasks, and the object believes that more than one instance of this object exists, though the I.A.M. is uncertain if one of its instances is resided within the Backrooms.

McDonal Milkshak (Joke).title: McDonal Milkshak (Joke)
McDonal Milkshak (Joke).description:
The McDonal Milkshak is a liquid substance that is so utterly difficult to obtain, that only one wanderer has been able to consume this liquid. However, for some exotic logic, we know basically everything about it, and we are here to describe this deity-like fluid. McDonal Milkshak has a variety of colorations and flavors, although the most eminent is the strawberry Milkshak, it is identical to its counterpart from reality and few to no alterations are made to the liquid itself or the vial containing the fluid, as far as taste goes, it's indistinguishable from an actual McDonalds Milkshake, though it is 100% better than an actual McDonalds Milkshake. It has been demonstrated that whatever holds this object becomes indestructible, nonetheless, the thing that possesses the substance can still be tempered by the liquid substance itself, such as cartons which become damp until completely deliquesced, once this happens the fluid will shift to another location, attempting to track them are futile as trackers malfunction once attached to the thing maintaining the liquid substance.

It is incoherent where this liquid comes from, as there are no significant correlations to the start of its existence, it's thought to be the first noclipped liquid, and due to the effects of the Backrooms, an innocent McDonald Milkshake got turned into this item. However, regardless of this "new" instances of the Mcdonal Milkshak have been noticed, so it is possible that something is generating it, though it is still yet a highly arduous assignment to obtain this item, as instances of McDonal Milkshak's are normally in very, but VERY hazardous levels, which were probably documented by a 10-year-old. Nonetheless, you must traverse a highly unsecure level in order to obtain the liquid, here are an agenda of challenges must go through these include but are not limited to —

*Entity-Infested Levels
*Unfunny Joke Pages
*Deadzone Levels.
*and Much More.

There are other obstacles in the way to gaining a McDonal Milkshak. Although, the documenter of the page can't be bothered to add them.
- Some Dude that's totally not related to the lore of the page creator. 5/10/20

Anyhow, let's give a bit more description of this item. The McDonal Milkshake can be blended with most liquid-like objects, causing a chemical reaction, so powerful it can obliterate, not kill, Obliterate most entities within the outburst range. Indeed, the reaction is so unstable and powerful to the point that both liquid substances shatter, forming a 50 feet radius blast, so it's recommended to NOT combine any substance with this object unless you have some way of fast transportation. Despite this, it would be a misuse of such an extraordinarily infrequent object. Something noteworthy to state is that this item should not be consumed, as anyone who drinks this will transcend past the ninth reality, which is an extremely dangerous and hostile place to exist within, a normal human body is incapable of surviving in this state and would be instantly crushed into a great grand stellated 120 cell within 0.23 second.
McDonal Milkshak (Joke).lead:
Object M: "McdonalMil "www.MEG.com/Mcdonal MilkshakㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤMarch 14th, 2018
----
----

The Mcdonal Milkshak is the most powerful substance liquid among the Backrooms or even the universe.

Photography of the Mcdonal Milkshak; Photographer presumed death.

Object 3.title: Object 3
Object 3.description:
Object 3 is a green, highly corrosive, and toxic substance found in very specific levels. Attempts to store the liquid in jars or containers are usually infeasible, as the substance dissolves a vast majority of materials.

The most defining trait of Object 3 is its ability to "infect" levels. Once exposed to one, it'll spread outwards by decaying the level's structure at a slow, but persistent rate, and eventually transform the entire space into a warped, infected mass. With nothing to obstruct it, the infection will eventually seep all throughout the level with ease.

The most extreme case of the infection is Level 630—being the source of the substance. As a result, any entrances or exits leading to or from Level 630, or any infected level in general, effectively become vulnerable points for Object 3 to bleed through. Additional infected levels include Level 22 and Level 64, both compromised by sharing an entrance or exit with Level 630. Levels not having a direct entrance or exit to an infected level cannot be infected, unless if the substance is manually moved to another level.

Object 32.title: Object 32
Object 32.description:
🗕 🗗 🗙
----
← → ↻ ⌂🔓︎ www.MEG.com/action=search

>>search M.E.G. records...

>object-32

>>5 results for 'object 32'

*Object 4

*Object 8

*Object 16

*Object 32

*Object 64

>open file: object-32: corruptium

>>executing file...

>>..8%...16%...64%...88%...98%...99%...

Danger Level: Hazardous

>> Number Designation: 32

>> Object Classification: Unbeneficial for Human Use

>> Location: Heavily Unstable Levels

Object 32, known as corruptium, or scientifically as Substum ins, is an unstable and unpredictable substance found commonly in unstable and corrupted levels of the Backrooms. Up until February 22, 2026, Object 32 was referred to as "corruptionite", a temporary placeholder name that has since been dropped in favor for its current more appropriate name.

Properties

Contrary to popular belief, corruptium does not possess the ability to corrupt levels or mutate entities but is rather a byproduct of instability and corruption. Corruptium is extremely hot, reaching up to approximately 1,200°C or 2,192°F degrees, which is capable of giving wanderers 3rd-degree burns or fatal injuries in just seconds and is comparable to molten lava.

It is generally believed that the instability, the high density, and the pressure are the reasons for its high temperature. Due to how hot corruptium is, storing the liquid in plastic cups or bottles by all means is not possible. Accordingly, it is best to store it in metallic cups or containers in a cool environment. Corruptium is very viscous, akin to ketchup, meaning one can easily escape the liquid if it is flowing to them.

Furthermore, if corruptium successfully coats an entire object, the materials making up the object will be permanently replaced with the aforementioned corruptium. Corruptium can manifest as a liquid or a solid, but instances of it being a gas or plasma have not been reported. Unlike in liquid form, corruptium is mostly harmless in solid form, and will match the temperature of its surroundings. Corruptium only melts back to liquid form when heated pressure is applied to it.
Usage
Although unadvised, one possible application for corruptium could be using it as a weapon against entities. Otherwise, there are no cases where it should be used due to its hazardous properties.
Identification
corruptium can be identified via the heat it emits, which one can feel as far as 30 feet away from it. corruptium's appearance is interpreted as TV static, though more colorful. Moreover, corruptium can appear as rectangular blocks of flashing neon over grey, purple, blue, or violet. Danger Level: Hazardous

>> Number Designation: 32

>> Object Classification: Unbeneficial for Human Use

>> Location: Heavily Unstable Levels

Object 32, known as corruptium, or scientifically as Substum ins, is an unstable and unpredictable substance found commonly in unstable and corrupted levels of the Backrooms. Up until February 22, 2026, Object 32 was referred to as "corruptionite", a temporary placeholder name that has since been dropped in favor for its current more appropriate name.

Properties

Contrary to popular belief, corruptium does not possess the ability to corrupt levels or mutate entities but is rather a byproduct of instability and corruption. Corruptium is extremely hot, reaching up to approximately 1,200°C or 2,192°F degrees, which is capable of giving wanderers 3rd-degree burns or fatal injuries in just seconds and is comparable to molten lava.

It is generally believed that the instability, the high density, and the pressure are the reasons for its high temperature. Due to how hot corruptium is, storing the liquid in plastic cups or bottles by all means is not possible. Accordingly, it is best to store it in metallic cups or containers in a cool environment. Corruptium is very viscous, akin to ketchup, meaning one can easily escape the liquid if it is flowing to them.

Furthermore, if corruptium successfully coats an entire object, the materials making up the object will be permanently replaced with the aforementioned corruptium. Corruptium can manifest as a liquid or a solid, but instances of it being a gas or plasma have not been reported. Unlike in liquid form, corruptium is mostly harmless in solid form, and will match the temperature of its surroundings. Corruptium only melts back to liquid form when heated pressure is applied to it.
Usage
Although unadvised, one possible application for corruptium could be using it as a weapon against entities. Otherwise, there are no cases where it should be used due to its hazardous properties.
Identification
corruptium can be identified via the heat it emits, which one can feel as far as 30 feet away from it. corruptium's appearance is interpreted as TV static, though more colorful. Phenomenon 2.title: Phenomenon 2
Phenomenon 2.description:
BACK-NET (Phenomenon 2) is essentially the Backrooms’ version of the internet; a wide area network that uses an anomalous protocol in order to transfer data between devices. Access points to BACK-NET are usually exclusive to levels mimicking manmade structures such as Level 0, Level 4, or Level 9, making long-distance communication difficult outside of the few levels with the connection.

However, unlike the baseline Frontrooms internet, BACK-NET holds various anomalous properties that distinguish it from a normal wireless network, with the network being mysterious in nature and often quite unstable at times. Research by The Kauer Research Organization into BACK-NET is ongoing to this very day.
